programme.title: Spy Next Door
programme.desc: A former CIA agent hoping to settle down with his girlfriend uses an opportunity to babysit her children as a chance to win them over. However, things take a dramatic turn when an old enemy shows up, forcing him back into his old lifestyle.

programme.title: In Time
programme.desc: Justin Timberlake and Amanda Seyfried co-star in this sci-fi thriller set in a future world where aging stops at 25, and the only way for one man to stay alive is to steal more time.

programme.title: The Last Witch Hunter
programme.desc: The last witch hunter (Vin Diesel) is all that stands between humanity and the combined forces of the most horrifying witches in history.

programme.title: The Big Short
programme.desc: Based on the true story of four outsiders who saw what the big banks, media, and government refused to: the global collapse of the economy. A bold investment leads them into the dark underbelly of banking, where everyone and everything is in question.

programme.title: Bandidas
programme.desc: In turn-of-the-century Mexico, two very different women become a bank-robbing duo in an effort to combat a ruthless enforcer terrorizing their town.

programme.title: Benji
programme.desc: Two school kids strike up a friendship with an orphaned puppy named Benji. When danger befalls them and they end up kidnapped by robbers who are in over their heads, Benji and his scruffy sidekick come to the rescue.

programme.title: Memoirs Of An Invisible Man
programme.desc: When a government experiment goes awry it renders an innocent bystander invisible.

programme.title: Snitch
programme.desc: A father (Dwayne Johnson) goes undercover for the DEA in order to free his son who was imprisoned after being set up in a drug deal.

programme.title: Prince Valiant
programme.desc: In a time now lost in the mists of memory, the great King Arthur rules in the legendary citadel that is Camelot. Starring: Joanna Lumley, Stephen Moyer, Katherine Heigl, Thomas Kretschmann

programme.title: Blood Waters of Dr. Z
programme.desc: Seeking revenge against those responsible for stripping him of his tenure and the denial of human research subjects, Dr. Z mutates himself into a hideous — and murderous — half-human-half-fish-like creature!

programme.title: Cave Dwellers
programme.desc: Newly-married wife Jenni can't tell if the skull she sees in her new home is a ghostly apparition of her husband's dead first wife, hallucinations from her own nervous condition... or something else.

programme.title: The Rebel Set
programme.desc: Three coffee shop customers help an owner rob an armored car.

programme.title: Hercules Unchained
programme.desc: While trying to resolve a quarrel between warring brothers Eteocles and Polynices, Hercules is taken captive by Queen Omphale of Lydia. Can he escape from the royal court and rescue the woman he loves, before it’s too late?

programme.title: Indestructible Man
programme.desc: In Indestructible Man, a violent criminal named Butcher Benson is executed and becomes indestructible when he’s brought back to life. Butcher seeks revenge against those who caused his execution.

programme.title: Girl in Gold Boots
programme.desc: A girl from rural California goes to Los Angeles to work as a go-go dancer, but soon gets involved with drug dealers.

programme.title: Giant Spider Invasion
programme.desc: Mike and the 'bots suffer through The Giant Spider Invasion (1975).

programme.title: Hercules And The Captive Women
programme.desc: Hercules and the Captive Women: Hercules tries to save his son from an evil queen.

programme.title: The Crawling Eye
programme.desc: Things start to look eerily familiar to scientists Crevett and Brooks when mountain climbers begin losing their heads in the Trollenberg Mountains.

programme.title: King Dinosaur
programme.desc: In King Dinosaur, the crew pokes fun as scientists are terrorized by snakes, bugs, and dinosaurs on a newly discovered planet.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: The Superfantastic Mega-Buds / It Came From My Nose
programme.desc: McGee, Gretchen and Squirt get super fantastic mega powers!
programme.sub-title: The Superfantastic Mega-Buds / It Came From My Nose

programme.title: My Goldfish is Evil: Stinker!
programme.desc: Inspired by the horrible mess that Beanie has cooked up for his mother's breakfast, Admiral Bubbles decides to cook up a garbage monster, bringing it to life to do his evil bidding and to terrorize the town.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Marshmallow Madness / 28 Suzi's Later
programme.desc: Armand goes into a feeding frenzy when wild marshmallows give the campers a rash.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Head Two Head / The Last of the Wild
programme.desc: Suzi and Gretchen get fused into a two-headed Gruzi!
programme.sub-title: Head Two Head / The Last of the Wild

programme.title: My Goldfish is Evil: Ghostfish!
programme.desc: When Beanie's Mom joins Mrs. Prehistoritch in a comical séance to ask Mrs. Prehistoritch's late husband Ernie about the location of her favorite golf ball, the proceedings give the Goldfish an idea.

programme.title: My Goldfish is Evil: Rescued!
programme.desc: In celebration of "Earth Day", Kevin Kelp, an ex- Greenspace activist, has converted the city's old incinerator into a recycling plant. But this is a great disappointment to Admiral Bubbles, who hates to see the old incinerator go to waste.

programme.title: My Goldfish is Evil: Derailed!
programme.desc: While Beanie and his Nana are dropping off Mom at the train station, Admiral Bubbles temporarily escapes, and makes his way to the station control station where he discovers that an experimental amphibious Air Force jet is on one of the trains.

programme.title: My Goldfish is Evil: The Great Outdoors!
programme.desc: Beanie, his Mom, and Elwood head to family summer camp, and Beanie brings his evil pet to keep him under control. But the camp turns out to be run by a city-slicker who doesn't know the first thing about nature!

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Butt-Squad / You Sank My Battle-Squirt!
programme.desc: Buttsquat steps in to help the bottomdwellers when they suddenly acquire the same red hair and freckles as their Sunny Smiles nemesis. When an alien invasion threatens Camp Lakebottom, the kids defend the camp against the alien mothership!

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Are You My Mommy? / Slimey Come Home
programme.desc: The Campers and Buttsquat come across an ancient pyramid inhabited by mummy's.
programme.sub-title: Are You My Mommy? / Slimey Come Home

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Cabin Fever / It's a Horrible Life
programme.desc: While lost in the woods, McGee, Gretchen, Squirt and Sawyer discover a creepy cabin.
programme.sub-title: Cabin Fever / It's a Horrible Life

programme.title: My Goldfish is Evil: Grade-A Goldfish!
programme.desc: When Beanie fails a test at school, Admiral Bubbles convinces him to let him plant a small transceiver in his mouth so that Admiral Bubbles can transmit Beanie the answers to his next test.

programme.title: My Goldfish is Evil: Forgetful Fish!
programme.desc: Beanie and Elwood have done their school science report on earthquakes, discovering that the town is built on a fault line. Admiral Bubbles realizes that with a simple machine serving as a catalyst, he could easily destroy the entire town.

programme.title: My Goldfish is Evil: Monstro-Crane of Doom
programme.desc: My Goldfish is Evil! Chronicles the hilarious adventures of a boy named Beanie and his tiny pet goldfish Admiral Bubbles. The twist? Admiral Bubbles has evil plans to conquer the world.

programme.title: My Goldfish is Evil: Lights, Camera, Goldfish!
programme.desc: When Admiral Bubbles tricks Beanie into emailing pages from his diary to the producers of the "Detechnoman Movie", they mistakenly assume that the outrageous tales are a pitch for the script, and he is soon hired to work on the film.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Bird Brains / The Real Vincent
programme.desc: Squirt is eager to assist his idol, Dr. Sapstein on his latest quest for the rare mocker bird.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Ring Around The Gretchen / Chilli Con Carnage
programme.desc: Beauty pageant Gretchen looks to connect with her girly side.
programme.sub-title: Ring Around The Gretchen / Chilli Con Carnage

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Sweat, Hot Lakebottom Summer / The S'Mogre
programme.desc: A heat wave forces McGee to sneak a dab of Sawyer's anti-sweat cream. After trying to cheer up Armand, McGee creates a s'more so big, it topples and coats him in delicious goo.
programme.sub-title: Sweat, Hot Lakebottom Summer / The S'Mogre

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Fright Club / Bottomdome
programme.desc: The campers discover an enchanted flashlight.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Pirate of Ickygloomy / Attack of the 50-Foot Squirt
programme.desc: While playing Pirates on a shipwreck the Bottom Dwellers must battle the ghost Spitbeard the Pirate.
programme.sub-title: Pirate of Ickygloomy / Attack of the 50-Foot Squirt

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Pod Parents / Zombie Scouts
programme.desc: Suzi, desperate to get McGee sent home, invites her parents for a visit to Camp Lakebottom.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Itchy Witchy Pizza Project / Breakout From Buttvault B
programme.desc: Gretchen, McGee and Squirt get trapped by witch who forces the campers into pizza slave labour.
programme.sub-title: Itchy Witchy Pizza Project / Breakout From Buttvault B

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Pranks for Nothing / Zombie Dearest
programme.desc: Eager to regain his lost prank mojo, McGee releases a mischievous pixy named Hanky Pranky.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Red Drawn / Pandora's Jock
programme.desc: A travelling salesman visits Lakebottom one boring rainy day and sells McGee a magic pencil.

programme.title: My Goldfish is Evil: The Secret Terractor!
programme.desc: During summer vacation, Beanie is spending two weeks on his Grandpa and Grandma's farm, and brings Admiral Bubbles along so he can keep an eye on him.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Scare-A-Normal-Activity / Beast Feast
programme.desc: McGee vows to protect Lakebottom's secrets from the prying host of 'Paranormal Confronter.'

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Who's Ghouling Who? / The Adominable Dr. Squatch
programme.desc: McGee accidentally summons Percy, a ghost with a very 'fiery' personality.
programme.sub-title: Who's Ghouling Who? / The Adominable Dr. Squatch

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Meet The Gretch's Parents / Now with 100% More Portal
programme.desc: McGee and Squirt are flabbergasted when Gretchen's parents turn out to be the famous action-star couple, the Gritchersons! The kids discover an interdimensional portal to travel back and forth between parallel Lakebottom dimensions.
programme.sub-title: Meet The Gretch's Parents / Now with 100% More Portal

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Duh'Rehka / Chore Leave
programme.desc: While chasing his gopher-nemesis, Sawyer gets zapped into his brain bin and emerges super smart!

programme.title: My Goldfish is Evil: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under The Fishbowl!
programme.desc: An impending ecological disaster involving a sunken oil tanker on the lip of an underwater volcano sends the city into a panic. Nia's uncle Captain Abbott is to lead an submarine mission to raise the tanker before it can explode.
programme.sub-title: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under The Fishbowl!

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: The Day Squirt Stood Still / Anti Gravity
programme.desc: The campers track down a UFO inhabited by an alien from the planet Squipiter.
programme.sub-title: The Day Squirt Stood Still / Anti Gravity

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Last Days / Knock on Wood
programme.desc: McGee tries to save Camp Lakebottom from being overtaken by the Grim Camper.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Messy Bessie / Nanny Num Nums
programme.desc: The campers build a race car to help Sawyer relive his pro-racing days.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: It's a Headless Horse, Man / Voyage to the Bottom of the Deep
programme.desc: McGee is thrilled when he wins a mounted horse head.
programme.sub-title: It's a Headless Horse, Man / Voyage to the Bottom of the Deep

programme.title: My Goldfish is Evil: Space Command, We Have A Goldfish!

programme.sub-title: Space Command, We Have A Goldfish!

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Adventures in Bottomland / Golem My Way?

programme.sub-title: Adventures in Bottomland / Golem My Way?

programme.title: My Goldfish is Evil: Goldfish Teriyaki!
programme.desc: A huge battle between Beanie and Admiral Bubbles in the kitchen results in the destruction of the oven. When Mom discovers this, she decides to use the money she was saving to buy a new bed to buy an oven instead.

programme.desc: Can you share the task of driving, change places while driving or push the driver out while moving at regular speed? Can you prevent cats from defecating in your garden?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 202
programme.desc: Can certain sound frequencies persuade people that an area is haunted? Is it really possible to smell fear? Is it really as easy to move and bury a dead body as it is in the movies?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 9
programme.desc: Myths tested: Was it possible to survive an escape from Alcatraz? Does a duck's quack echo? Does the government implant secret chips in people that stud finders can detect?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 187
programme.desc: If a pressure-triggered bomb is cooled with liquid nitrogen, can its detonation be delayed long enough for a person to seek protection?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 204
programme.desc: Are people actually well-acquainted with the backs of their own hands? Can a needle be thrown through a glass pane without shattering it? Is it really impossible to ride a bicycle underwater?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 90
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can someone's hat be shot off? Is it possible to break someone out of jail with horses? Can an airbag turn a lock-pick into a lethal spear?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 128
programme.desc: Could a skydiver whose parachute failed to open hit a playground seesaw and send a small girl flying seven stories high...and she could still survive?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 117
programme.desc: Can a conventional electric stun gun be turned into a water-powered electric stun gun? Is firewalking real? Can a fire be put out by placing a fire extinguisher into it?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 52
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a bullet fired straight up in the air kill someone on the way back down? If low-end vodka is charcoal-filtered, will it become a top-shelf vodka?

programme.title: Meet Dave
programme.desc: A crew of miniature aliens operate a spaceship that has a human form

programme.title: Life or Something Like It
programme.desc: The perfect world of a beautiful Seattle television reporter is shattered by the prediction of her death in seven days.

programme.title: Superhero Movie
programme.desc: An orphaned teenager is bitten by a radioactive dragonfly, granting him superhuman powers in this parody of films like Spider-Man, X-Men, and Fantastic Four.

programme.title: What Happens In Vegas
programme.desc: Two strangers who drunkenly get married in Vegas fight over a huge jackpot.

programme.title: My Super Ex-Girlfriend
programme.desc: When he breaks up with his girlfriend, he discovers his ex is actually the reluctant superhero, G Girl. A scorned woman, she unleashes her super powers to humiliate and torment him.

programme.title: Joe Somebody
programme.desc: Tim Allen is Joe, a guy whose life has been steadily changing for the worse. When the office bully goes too far, Joe decides it's time to turn himself from a nobody into a somebody.

programme.title: Cold Souls: Cold Souls
programme.desc: Paul is an actor who feels bogged down by his participation in a production of Chekov's play, Vanya.

programme.title: Bedazzled
programme.desc: Elliot Richards is a hapless young man consumed by unrequited love, and desperate to change his life, he makes a deal with the devil who slyly promises to do just that — for a price.

programme.title: 12 In A Box
programme.desc: 12 In A Box is the laugh-out-loud story of 12 former school friends who get together for a reunion they will never forget. Staring Miranda Hart.

programme.title: King Of California
programme.desc: Commercial airline pilot Whip Whitaker (Denzel Washington) has a problem with drugs and alcohol, though so far he's managed to complete his flights safely.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback makes a device to control DM's car.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold are chosen as ambassadors to meet space aliens.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Rent a Butler
programme.desc: Duckula sells Igor and Nanny to an agency to make some money

programme.title: Count Duckula: Return of the Curse of the Secret of the Mummy's Tomb Meets Franken Duckula's Monster.
programme.desc: Duckula is chased by an alien cabbage, a werewolf, a mummy, Frankenstein's monster, Von Goosewing, and some angry villagers.
programme.sub-title: Return of the Curse of the Secret of the Mummy's Tomb Meets Franken Duckula's Monster.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Ducknapped
programme.desc: A ransom note is sent to Castle Duckula saying that Count Duckula has been kidnapped,.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback puts Danger Mouse and Penfold in his dream machine, where Penfold's random thoughts become reality, usually to the detriment of Danger Mouse.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback steals a growth serum from Professor von Squawkencluck and with it creates giant chickens to take over the world once and for all. And for once, Penfold saves the day!

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold travel to Scotland to keep Greenback from destroying the world with music from all the bagpipes in the world.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold must face the demon of the fourth dimension. Danger Mouse is transported onto a This Was Your Life television show.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback has a device that transmits waves to brainwash people. He brainwashes Colonel K to send Danger Mouse and Penfold on a mission to the South Pole, where they have trouble with a mechanical wild goose that lays explosive eggs.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Castle Duckula: Open to the Public
programme.desc: Duckula decides to hold an open day at Castle Duckula.
programme.sub-title: Castle Duckula: Open to the Public

programme.title: Count Duckula: Mississippi Duck
programme.desc: Count Duckula and his servants travel onto a steamboat in the River Mississippi.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold go to the Amazon jungle where a tribe have been sacrificing people to The Great Ant God from Ataxia. Penfold is captured by the tribe and his "magic caterpillars" (eyebrows) are to be sacrificed.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Dear Diary
programme.desc: Duckula and Goosewing discover their grandfathers' diaries, and history is sure to repeat itself!

programme.title: Count Duckula: Vampire Vacation
programme.desc: Duckula travels to Spain to meet his arsonist cousin and fights the deadliest bull in Spain.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold are sent to find Merlin's magic inkwell which will grant the finder a wish. They go on a treasure hunt by following a series of clues.
programme.sub-title: Where There's a Well There's a Way

programme.title: Count Duckula: Town Hall Terrors
programme.desc: Duckula goes to the Town Hall to get a grant for his crumbling castle.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Astro Duck
programme.desc: Duckula's new computer causes problems for TRASH and ends up landing the castle on the moon.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: An old English manor was moved away and held for ransom. Danger Mouse and Penfold meet with members of the Building Location and Emergency Expedition Platoon (B.L.E.E.P.) to recover it.
programme.sub-title: One of our Stately Homes Is Missing

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Crumhorn invents a chemical that allows him to create land sharks to terrorize London. Things get even more complicated when Penfold eats some of the formula.

programme.title: Count Duckula: The Lost City of Atlantis
programme.desc: Count Duckula and co. are stuck on a tiny island.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold head to outer space where they find a station has been beaming darkness onto the Earth. On the station they encounter a fearsome gremlin which operates on reverse logic.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Space alien J. J. Quark announces that he is going to claim the Earth as his.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Dr Crumhorn returns and uses his transformation pills to turn himself into a little girl in an attempt to persuade Danger Mouse and Penfold to help her out and eventually lead them to break into Fort Knox.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Colonel K thinks Danger Mouse has camouflaged himself and starts rambling. After Danger Mouse arrives to straighten up things, Danger Mouse and Penfold head to Canada to track down a Bigfoot that has been crushing the local villages and landscape.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Venice a Duck Not a Duck
programme.desc: In Venice, the Egg plans to get his revenge on Duckula.
programme.sub-title: Venice a Duck Not a Duck

programme.title: Count Duckula: Restoration Comedy
programme.desc: Bored with the look of the castle, Duckula hires a designer to redecorate the castle.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold head to the North Pole on news that Baron Greenback and Stiletto have been selling over a thousand heat lamps to the Eskimos and causing the polar ice caps to melt.
programme.sub-title: I Spy with My Little Eye...

programme.title: Count Duckula: Transylvanian Homesick Blues
programme.desc: Duckula, Igor and Nanny take a rollercoaster ride through time...literally!

programme.title: Count Duckula: Manhattan Duck
programme.desc: Castle Duckula mistakedly lands in the middle of New York.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Sawdust Ring
programme.desc: Duckula, Igor and Nanny join the circus.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Bloodsucking Fruit Bats of the Lower Amazon
programme.desc: After Fang the Bat's training by Igor as a blood-sucking bat is ruined by a preferred taste for choccy-biccies.
programme.sub-title: Bloodsucking Fruit Bats of the Lower Amazon

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold journey to the Moon to put a radio jamming station Greenback has built there out of operation. While there, Greenback has Stiletto fight them off with some remote controlled mechanical monsters and weapons.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Mobile Home
programme.desc: Thinking he is rebuliding the castle, Duckula accidentally sells it to The Crow Brothers in disguise, brick by brick!

programme.title: Count Duckula: Bad Luck Duck
programme.desc: Duckula experiences some real bad luck on Friday the 13th

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold head to the Indian Ocean to investigate several ship sinkings caused by "ghost buses". The buses turn out to be ice blocks shaped like a London bus created by Baron Greenback.
programme.sub-title: The Strange Case of the Ghost Bus

programme.title: Count Duckula: There are Werewolves at the Bottom of Our Garden
programme.desc: The castle's werewolf has escaped and it's up to Igor and Nanny to get it back before Duckula finds out.
programme.sub-title: There are Werewolves at the Bottom of Our Garden

programme.title: Count Duckula: O.O. Duck
programme.desc: The Egg decides to steal Castle Duckula to take over the world.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Private Beak
programme.desc: Duckula becomes a private detective in Chicago.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Due to a fluke in the narrator's microphone, Danger Mouse and Penfold are transported to the year 1215, where they have to act out characters from Robin Hood.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Colonel K. is kidnapped by a mechanical cat named P.A.W.S.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Colonel K teams Danger Mouse up with a robot Penfold, and Penfold with a robot Danger Mouse. The robots prove to be a lot of trouble for the real ones, taking instructions too literally or getting stuck running in circles.
programme.sub-title: Four Heads Are Better Than Two

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: A giant spaceship is spotted heading for Earth. It's part of the "Intergalactic 147" snooker game and it wants to pot the Earth into a black hole. This is the series finale.

programme.title: Count Duckula: Hi-Duck
programme.desc: Duckula, Igor and Nanny take an airplane to Nice.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Penfold and Danger Mouse are shocked to find a giant spider in their bathroom.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 3
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a two bedroomed mid terraced in Stoke-on-Trent, a three bedroomed house in Hampshire with no roof and a semi detached house in Leeds.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 1
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit a two bedroom semi in Netherton, a flat in south London and a former shop in Tiverton.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and Lucy Alexander look at a run-down house in Derby and a plot of land in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 15
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a property in London, a bungalow in Hampshire and a cottage in Norfolk.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy view properties in Margate, Harrogate and London.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 1
programme.desc: Lucy & Martin travel to Yorkshire to see a 1970s semi which may hold the key to the potential in some nearby barns! In Manchester there's a terrace going for a knock down price.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 6
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit a property in Dulwich West London, a house in Kent that's taken a drop in price and a property in Cornwall.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 20
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit a detached house in Wiltshire, a three-bed house in Salford and a property in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy view properties in Harrogate, Stoke and London.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 16
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a curious building in Stoke-on-Trent, a flat in Crystal Palace fit for a Queen and a four bedroom house in Glamorgan.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 6
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a money-making mid-terrace house in Crew, Cheshire, a lot for a tiny amount in Oxfordshire and finally, an opportunity to be grabbed near the beautiful seaside town of Torquay.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 26
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a house in Blackburn, revisit a house in Sheerness in Kent and an old pub in Derby.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit properties in Liverpool, Norfolk and Devon.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 19
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit somewhere with lots of potential in Devon, a large property in Wales and a house in Telford, Shropshire.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 20
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit a three bed end-of-terrace in Darlington, a property in Fulham, London and a two bed terraced house in Burnley.

programme.title: The Shape of Things to Come
programme.desc: Sometime in the future, man has set up colonies on the Moon, when Earth becomes uninhabitable. A madman decides to destroy the Moon colonies with his robots and automated ships, and only three people and their robot can stop him.

programme.title: Futtock's End
programme.desc: In this silent-style comedy, General Futtock hosts a weekend at his country estate. Chaos erupts as the butler (Sir Michael Hordern) spies on guests, rum spills into the punch, and a lost Japanese tourist adds to the mayhem. Hilarity ensues.

programme.title: Come Play with Me
programme.desc: A health-resort where both the clients and the employees easily take their clothes off and have a little fun is the setting of this sex-comedy. Starring: Irene Handl, Alfie Bass. Guinness Book Of World Records lists it's 210 week cinematic run

programme.title: Blue Hour
programme.desc: The Blue Hour tells the story of Tanya, a young woman with a mysterious past, who finds herself trapped in a sexual hell in Los Angeles. Punctuated by striking visuals, The Blue Hour is a lost masterpiece of late period sexploitation.

programme.title: Naked as Nature Intended
programme.desc: Naked As Nature Intended sees four young beauties enjoying the sights of the West Country while exploring the joys of nudism. A nudie-cutie feature from the early days of naturism!

programme.title: The Baby
programme.desc: Twisted family drama with shocking horror elements.

programme.title: Cool It Carol
programme.desc: Two small-town teenagers move to London's West End and get involved in prostitution and pornography before tiring of big-city life. Starring: Robin Askwith, Janet Lynn, Jess Conrad

programme.title: Erik The Viking
programme.desc: Erik the Viking and his men travel across the sea to find Valhalla to ask the gods to end the Age of Ragnarok.

programme.title: Summer of Fear
programme.desc:
A teenage girl's(Linda Blair) life is turned upside down after her cousin moves into her house, and as time goes by she begins to suspect that she may be a practitioner of witchcraft.
Director: Wes Craven

programme.title: Nosferatu
programme.desc: Vampire Count Orlok expresses interest in a new residence and real estate agent Hutter's wife.

programme.title: Battle Beyond The Stars
programme.desc: A young farmer sets out to recruit mercenaries to defend his peaceful planet, which is under threat of invasion by the evil tyrant Sador and his armada of aggressors. Starring: George Peppard, Robert Vaughn, Richard Thomas

programme.title: Hunter
programme.desc: When Rick Hunter, a renegade cop who takes justice into his own hands, partners with the stunning and rebellious Sgt. McCall, they set out to crack down on L.A.'s slimiest criminals.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Love Gone Wrong
programme.desc: Love can be beautiful, love can be blind and love can be deadly. Desire and jealousy are powerful forces driving women to dark and horrifying places in their minds.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Teen Killers
programme.desc: They were too young to drink or vote but they were not too young to commit the most brutal of crimes. Some were driven by pure curiosity: how would it feel to take a life?

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: This episode transports us into the teenage territory of Indiana pack leader Melinda Loveless, whose jealousy sparks a rampage that leads to the kidnap and torture of a 12-year-old.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Twisted Thrills
programme.desc: The intimate thoughts of a twisted mind would be enough to stun a community and horrify the world. But thoughts alone may not be enough to turn someone into a killer. This is a bizarre journey into the world of women who kill for twisted thrills.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Lethal Love
programme.desc: Love is a powerful emotion, one that sits in the core of our soul and fills our lives with hope. But when love turns sour, the results can be deadly. In an act of revenge, these deadly women unite with one common goal, to kill.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Sacrifice Their Blood
programme.desc: To the outside world these caring mothers present a face of love - a suburban Mom, a caring community member, a housewife - but when the doors close, these deadly women will do whatever it takes to have their own needs met, even destroy their family.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Love Sick
programme.desc: Young love can be a beautiful time, but when jealousy raises its ugly head it can also be deadly. These baby-faced schoolgirls looked pure and innocent, but don’t be deceived.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Lover's Revenge
programme.desc: These women burn madly with anger so deep they'll kill to satisfy it. What makes them turn on the ones they love? Find out as we explore the reasons for a lover's revenge.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Breaking Point
programme.desc: Anger, fear and despair are basic human emotions. But when these emotions are coupled with a broken mind, a perfect storm is created. Teetering on the edge of unbalance, these women resort to murder when pushed to the breaking point.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: These deadly women were consumed by obsessions that overtook their minds and their lives. These women all had a fatal fixation.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: No one doubts a woman’s capacity to love, but sometimes lust can turn lethal. In the ultimate fatal attraction, "Deadly Women" looks at cases of murder driven by obsessive love.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: A rifle in a shopping mall, a syringe of potassium chloride and a syringe of insulin, each in the hands of a mentally disturbed woman, lead to the tragic end of many lives.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Married to Murder
programme.desc: To the outside world, their marriages seemed happy. But behind closed doors, there was no domestic bliss. These women wanted out, but getting a divorce wasn’t their preferred option.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: A lovely lady with an innocent smile, kind eyes, a charming personality? No one would suspect a woman like that would murder. These women don’t look or behave like killers.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Secrets and Lies
programme.desc: Behind their friendly smiles, deadly women hide histories of bloodshed and murder.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: While marriage is meant to be for life, it often ends in divorce. These wives took their marriages vow seriously: "till death do us part."

programme.title: Deadly Women: Greed
programme.desc: Known as “black widows,” these women kill their spouses, lovers and children for profit. Murder for them becomes a business. Their trade is murder. Their profit? Blood money.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Blood ties are strong ties, sometimes strong enough to pull a person into a murderous conspiracy. When a family member wields his or her power, the result can be death.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: These women never get their hands dirty: no blood touched them because they manipulate others to kill for them...but they didn't get away.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Without Remorse
programme.desc: A small town without any crime was turned inside out by the arrival of one dangerous stranger. Some killers are cold and calculating; others are impulsive, unpredictable, and hard to pin down.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Dishonored
programme.desc: Shirley Russell was determined enough to reach the rank of Captain in the United States Marine Corps. When this officer turned up missing, her colleagues refused to believe she had gone AWOL. It was unclear whether Captain Shirley Russell was alive.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The Killing Zone
programme.desc: In the 1990's the war on drugs was in full swing. The front lines were often America's inner cities where gangs use violence and murder to rule their empires.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Crime Spree
programme.desc: They swept through six states like a plague, thrill killers acting without rhyme or reason. They targeted adults and young girls, kidnapping, and murdering. And in their rampage, they left plenty of clues, but moving targets are the hardest to hit.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The World Trade Center Bombing
programme.desc: Terrorist bombings are what happen in other countries. Not here in the United States. And yet, in 1993, it happened in New York City.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Human Prey
programme.desc: Thomas Dillon killed five outdoorsmen at random over a period of three years in rural southern Ohio. After a tip led the FBI to the murder weapon, ballistic testing made a match.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The C-11 Squad
programme.desc: New York City is not stranger to gang violence. But when a 12-year-old child was kidnapped, the gangs had crossed a line. Law enforcement raced to find the missing boy before it was too late.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A daring kidnapping and a bizarre ransom note baffled Mississippi authorities. The victim was the wife of a wealthy businessman. A list of 12 names and a few drops of blood were the only clues to her fate.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Deadly Business
programme.desc: Many escape the poverty of India to start new lives in the United States. One turned the American dream into a nightmare. People started turning up dead in New York's Indian community-authorities struggled to link fraud and murder to a powerful man.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Blood Brothers
programme.desc: When the small community of Noel, Missouri discovered their bank was robbed, they're shocked. When the bank president turned up missing, they're outraged. And when clues began to surface the facts were more terrible than anyone could have guessed.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The Murdering Cowboy
programme.desc: The sun set on the Wild West long ago, but some people refused to notice. Claude Dallas was one of them. A wily trapper, Dallas typified the rugged individualism that forged this country. He went too far when he killed two game wardens in 1981.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The Predator
programme.desc: In the early 1990's, Washington, D.C., became the murder capital of the nation. Few were left untouched by gang violence, which was ignited by the drug trade. By 1994, that violence would strike at the very heart of law enforcement.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Dead Run
programme.desc: In Pennsylvania, an inmate escaped with the help of his female accomplice. Following a trail of fraudulent credit card receipts and deaths, authorities track the pair, but always find themselves one step behind.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Deadly Stranger
programme.desc: The body of a young woman found burned in a truck launched a manhunt to find her killer. When more bodies began to turn up across the country, the FBI believed the cases were linked. Investigators had a suspect. They just couldn't find him.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Small Town Terror
programme.desc: In a small Ohio community, an elderly woman and a teenaged boy disappeared. Because they vanished within hours of each other, police believed the cases were linked. But they didn't know how.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The True Story of Mississippi Burning
programme.desc: Philadelphia, Mississippi, 1964. A sleepy southern town steeped in tradition finds itself targeted for invasion.
programme.sub-title: The True Story of Mississippi Burning

programme.title: The FBI Files: Death in Alaska
programme.desc: The day began like any other for Cheryl and Paul Chapman, until the silence of a peaceful Sunday morning was shattered with a phone call.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: PS 2001
programme.desc: Mearth hates his first day of school on planet Ork so much that Mork and Mindy accompany him back to Ork Prep, where Mork's ancient teacher - who looks like a child - is still sternly in charge.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: Metamorphosis - The TV Show
programme.desc: Mindy, already worried about losing her job at the television station, foresees disaster when Mork and Mearth accidentally transfer minds, forcing her to take Mearth in Mork’s body to meet the new boss.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: Drive, She Said
programme.desc: Terror-stricken Mork believes the examiner assigned to give him a driving test is an incarnation of the devil, and careens along the test course in a spectacular tilted wheelie, with Mindy and Mearth in the car.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: Midas Mork
programme.desc: All that glitters is Mork's gold when the Orkan learns how to make the precious metal from polyester, buys Mindy a 60-room mansion where she can stable her new horse and hires tennis star Tracy Austin as son Mearth's tennis coach.

programme.title: Mork & Mindy: Gotta Run (Part 1)
programme.desc: Mork is overjoyed that he and Mindy can finally let it all hang out when he meets Kalnik - an alien from Neptune who apparently is also married to an earthling and the four seem to have everything in common.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: When a dishonest antique dealer sells Aunt Sally to the Piwakawaka Folklore Museum in New Zealand, Worzel Gummidge just has to follow.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: The Crowman finds out about a rival scarecrow-maker who has made the scarecrow ingredient 'mangel-worzel' scarce. Meanwhile Worzel himself goes missing.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: Worzel finds he has competition in the scarecrow business and doesn't fare much better when he ends up trying his hand at being a clothes prop.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: After being unwittingly left behind at a golf course, Worzel gets caught up in the efforts of The Captain and Aunt Sally culminating in the Annual Golf Championship Competition.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: Worzel completes a postponed Scarecrow Naming Ceremony and finds himself acclaimed as 'King of The Scarecrows' - which the Crowman is less than thrilled to hear about.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: Worzel's heads are finally sent on from England. Unfortunately, when the trunk label is eaten by a goat, they end up in the local folk museum - alongside Aunt Sally and a vintage fairground organ.
programme.sub-title: Ten Heads Are Better Than One

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: Worzel is hot on the heels of Travelling Scarecrowmaker but struggling to contend with his dark magic. Their encounter begins when Worzel is distracted by a funfair: can he reach Aunt Sally in time, and rescue her without his brave head?

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: The Travelling Scarecrow Maker seeks his revenge by kidnapping Worzel and transforming him into a Slave Scarecrow. With Aunt Sally next in his sights, can his evil plan be thwarted?

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: The Crowman recruits a reluctant Aunt Sally to rescue the enchanted Worzel. The task requires her to be a master of disguise to outwit the Travelling Scarecrow Maker - is she up to the challenge?

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: Worzel makes preparations for his Birthday Tea Dance. However, he soon discovers the guest list may prove distinctly lacking in attendees. Can The Crowman, Mickey and Manu come to the rescue?

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: Worzel's continuing efforts to win over Aunt Sally encounter a new obstacle when the Provincial Travelling Ballet troupe arrives in town.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: An impoverished Crowman is pursued by the bailiffs. Mickey and Manu decide to help but Worzel's efforts leave him stuck in an abandoned mine and, with the Crowman now retired, how will he escape?

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: Mickey, Manu and The Crowman prepare a funeral for the missing Worzel. The unfortunate scarecrow is still in the clutches of the Prospector who is finding him to be a less than helpful assistant.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: When Aunt Sally sees a poster for a beauty contest, she goes shopping for a posh dress, a 'tiararara' and a makeover. Alas, the beauty contest isn't all that it seemed.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: The Crowman has a new scarecrow who he assigns to Worzel to show him the ropes. Unfortunately, the new recruit proves to be rather reluctant which only gets worse when the crop sprayers arrive.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: When one of The Crowman's previous creations returns to visit his maker, Worzel develops a nasty case of the green-eyed monster - which only gets worse when Weevily Swede meets his Aunt Sally.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: Aunt Sally's greedy dreams of wealth lead Worzel, Mickey and Manu into trouble once more when she helps a waxwork pirate escape from a funfair side-show, in search of his long-buried treasure.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: After joining the Crowman at an art show, Aunt Sally is taken with the riches on offer for sale of a truly great work. Worzel, also, seeks fame and fortune - and intends to prove his own artistic talents accordingly.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: Worzel and Blighty are sent away as punishment, travelling by train. After seeing members of the crew breaking for tea and cake, their attempts to get some for themselves see the train leaving the station with no staff on board!

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: After joining the Crowman at an art show, Aunt Sally is taken with the riches on offer for sale of a truly great work. Worzel, also, seeks fame and fortune - and intends to prove his own artistic talents accordingly.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: A nearby convent presents the Crowman with a challenge: the commission of his first female scarecrow.

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge Down Under
programme.desc: New Zealand's governor general requires a scarecrow to prevent the eating of his beautiful lawns,so the crowman summons all of his greatest creations to assess who is best for the job.

programme.title: Happy Days: The Apartment

programme.title: Happy Days: Hard Cover

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard speaks with the pre-eminent investigators of the alleged crash of a UFO near Roswell, New Mexico in 1947.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: An investigation into the various conspiracy theories revolving around the tragic death of Diana, The People's Princess, in Paris in August 1997.
programme.sub-title: The Death of Princess Diana - Tragic Car Accident or Murder Most Foul?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Were the Mercury, Genesis, Apollo and Space Shuttle programs mere window dressing designed for public consumption?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: The legend of the werewolf throughout history and in various cultures is explored. The Conspiracy Show travels to England and rural Wisconsin to investigate claims of actual werewolf sightings.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Rise of the Fourth Reich (Nazi's in America)
programme.desc: The Allies defeated the Axis Powers on the battlefield, but did they actually win the war? Richard interviews several alternative historians who say that while the German Army may have surrendered in 1945, the Nazi's never officially did.
programme.sub-title: The Rise of the Fourth Reich (Nazi's in America)

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: A serious investigation of the long-held theory that Elvis Presley faked his death in August 1977. Jokes of Elvis sightings in Kalamazoo aside, there is a considerable volume of evidence to suggest Elvis may not have left the building

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Manchurian Candidates
programme.desc: Several prominent researchers in the field provide a short history of Mind Control experimentation beginning with the Nazis through to the CIA's infamous MK Ultra program.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Was Presidential hopeful, Robert F. Kennedy really killed by disgruntled Palestinian Sirhan Sirhan as we’ve been led to believe? Or was he another in a long line of Manchurian candidates, a victim of a long standing mind control program.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard witnesses a municipal employee in Toronto, Canada undergo a regression during which he re-experiences a previous life as a doctor in early 20 century England.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Does the Bible contain hidden messages encoded within certain passages of the Old Testament? Is the prophetic nature of these 'codes' proof the Bible's author is of supernatural or extraterrestrial origin?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard interviews the expedition organizer who is planning on leading a team of scientists, filmmakers and tourists to the Arctic aboard a Russian nuclear ice-breaker in search of an entrance into the interior of our world.
programme.sub-title: Journey to the Center of the Earth

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard meets members of the 9/11 Truth movement who challenge the official version of events concerning the attack on the U.S. Pentagon. Researchers present evidence to suggest the attack was perpetrated by some rogue element inside the U.S. government.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard investigates claims that the supposed mastermind behind the 9/11 terrorist attacks and leader of the Al Qaeda terrorist cell remained a CIA asset until his death.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard speaks with two investigative journalists and a natural health practitioner who maintain vaccines are neither safe nor effective.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Can radiation from electrical appliances, power lines, microwave ovens, and wireless technology such as cell phones, baby monitors, alter our DNA, and cause illnesses?
programme.sub-title: The Dangers of EMF (Electro Magnetic Frequencies)

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Alien Abductions
programme.desc: Richard speaks with two alleged alien abductees about their claims they were taken aboard an alien space craft.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: For centuries, farmers have been discovering strange, complex geometric patterns comprised of rings, circles and other intricate shapes in their fields.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard meets the owners of an abnormal humanoid skull allegedly discovered in Mexico in the 1930s and a researcher who has had the skull subjected to numerous tests including carbon-14 dating, and DNA in order to determine it’s origin.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Mystery of Oak Island
programme.desc: Richard investigates Canada's best-known mysteries, the Oak Island treasure. Also known as the money pit, the site has become one of the world's deepest and most costly archaeological digs.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Energy Healing
programme.desc: Several energy healers claim they are able to produce spontaneous miraculous healing through a laying on of hands. Claims of similar such healing by a man in a remote village in Brazil, known as John of God are also examined.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Execution of MLK and The Framing of James Earl Ray
programme.desc: In this re-examination of the assassination of civil rights leader Martin Luther King, Richard meets several researchers who provide evidence that the convicted gunman, James Earl Ray was not the real triggerman.
programme.sub-title: The Execution of MLK and The Framing of James Earl Ray

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Birthers - Obama's Birth Certificate
programme.desc: Richard interviews several prominent 'Birthers' who present evidence that proves Barack Obama is not eligible to be President because he does not meet the qualifications of a natural born citizen.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: RFID Chips
programme.desc: Could implantable RFID chips or Radio-frequency identification tags currently in widespread use in retail industry be used to surreptitiously track and control people?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Assassination of John Lennon
programme.desc: The Conspiracy Show travels to New York, LA and Toronto in order to investigate the murder of John Lennon. Several researchers posit that Lennon's supposed killer Mark David Chapman was either a patsy or a mind-controlled assassin.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Did Jim Morrison Fake His Death?
programme.desc: Is it conceivable that the Doors legendary front man did not die in a bathtub in Paris of an apparent drug overdose?
programme.sub-title: Did Jim Morrison Fake His Death?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Water Engine
programme.desc: Did the late Stanley Meyer figure out a way to run an internal combustion engine on pure water? And was he murdered because of the threat his invention posed to Big Oil?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Murder of Marilyn Monroe
programme.desc: Did this pop icon and blond bombshell succumb to a drug overdose in 1962 as we've been led to believe or was she killed for 'knowing too much?"

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Is The Cure for Cancer Being Suppressed?
programme.desc: Richard investigates claims that the medical profession, drug companies, the food industry and government agencies are suppressing a cure for cancer.
programme.sub-title: Is The Cure for Cancer Being Suppressed?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Bilderbergs
programme.desc: A glimpse backstage at the highly secretive annual gathering of the world's richest and most powerful movers and shakers.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Shroud of Turin
programme.desc: Is this famous piece of linen cloth the actual burial cloth of Jesus Christ or a clever Medieval hoax?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Fake Arab Spring?
programme.desc: Who was really behind a series of revolts, protests, and uprisings that swept the Middle East starting in late 2010?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Ouija Boards?
programme.desc: Harmless board game or portal to the spirit world? Richard delves into the history and folklore of 'talking boards' with a collector of Ouija boards and a world renowned paranormal investigator.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Global Warming, A Hoax?
programme.desc: Richard speaks with several journalists and researchers who argue the theory that human use of carbon-based fossil fuels will lead to catastrophic global warming.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: JFK
programme.desc: Richard meets with some prominent JFK assassination researchers to discuss the November 1963 murder of President John F. Kennedy.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Pterodactyls
programme.desc: Richard speaks with several explorers and cryptozoologists who believe pterodactyls, flying dinosaurs thought to have become extinct some 60 million years ago, are alive and thriving in remote places such as Papua New Guinea.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: GMO - Genetically Modified Food
programme.desc: Richard investigates claims crop plants created for human or animal consumption using the latest molecular biology techniques are unsafe.

programme.title: Butterfly Effect: Cook: In Pursuit of the Southern Islands
programme.desc: The story of Captain James Cook, renowned navigator, and his explorations of the Pacific and the discovery of Australia.
programme.sub-title: Cook: In Pursuit of the Southern Islands

programme.title: Butterfly Effect: Champollion: Unvravelling the Mysteries of the Stone
programme.desc: Champollion succeeds in deciphering the Rosetta Stone and solves the mystery of hieroglyphics.
programme.sub-title: Champollion: Unvravelling the Mysteries of the Stone

programme.title: Butterfly Effect: Gandhi and Decolonization
programme.desc: Portrait of the man, his thinking and his passive struggle against the British presence in India.

programme.title: Greatest Crimes of All Time: Hostage-Takers
programme.desc: For the police, hostage crises are the worst things that can happen. Under pressure from the public and the media, investigators must make snap decisions that mean the difference between life and death.

programme.title: Greatest Crimes of All Time: Woman Killers
programme.desc: Ted Bundy – maybe the most famous Woman Killer. But there are more. Ant they all create a perfect social environment in which no one recognizes their true nature.

programme.title: Greatest Crimes of All Time: Gang Bosses
programme.desc: They rule the underworld: gang bosses. They make millions with racketeering and drug trafficking. For investigators, it is more than a fight against criminality – it is a veritable war.

programme.title: Greatest Crimes of All Time: Angels of Death
programme.desc: In hospitals and nursing wards, death is an everyday occurrence. And that is precisely what they count on: angels of death. Some of them rack up more victims in this way than the worst serial killers in the world.

programme.title: Greatest Crimes of All Time: Serial Killers
programme.desc: Serial Killers victims are often selected at random. The murders have only one thing in common: the bodies are savagely disfigured. Hunting them leads investigators into a world of darkness.

programme.title: Greatest Crimes of All Time: Rampage Killers
programme.desc: Rampage Killers. They come out of nowhere – and when they occur, innocent people always die. Investigators must react in a flash and make difficult decisions. Every killing spree is a race against time.

programme.title: Greatest Crimes of All Time: Bank Robbers
programme.desc: Bank robbers. They are clever, planning their crimes for years. And if necessary, they even put human lives at risk. For investigators, each heist is different. But all robbers have one thing in common: they steal millions.

programme.title: Greatest Crimes of All Time: Female Murderers
programme.desc: Women usually kill in their own peculiar way: sophisticated, tactically, and from behind. They prefer tainted medicines, knives, or poison. And they often kill for years, unnoticed and in secret.

programme.title: Greatest Crimes of All Time: Hitmen
programme.desc: Hitmen are masters of covering their tracks. Their only concern is for loyalty and the greatest possible profit. Investigators must always take care not to become the next victim.
