programme.desc: Finally reaching Violet City, Ash’s search for the Gym is halted by a trip to the local Pokémon Academy. Ash does well playing teacher, until one pupil takes off with Pikachu!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: It’s finally time for Ash’s first Johto League Gym battle, and he’s feeling confident. But Falkner’s Flying-type Pokémon prove to be tough competition! Can Ash take the win—and his first Gym badge?

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Back on the road after his first Johto Gym badge win, Ash and the gang find a cute Marill that won’t stop crying! Nothing our heroes try will stop it, and a Team Rocket raid certainly won’t help!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Team Rocket’s latest invention is bound to be a hit...until Togepi and its newfound Sentret friend take it out for a spin!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Ash hears of a training ground for wild Charizard and decides it's just the place to hone his own Charizard’s strength. But the tough lessons to be learned there will change Ash and Charizard’s friendship forever!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Back on the road to the Azalea Gym, our heroes discover a Sunflora festival in a nearby town and meet a Trainer whose Sunflora is too sad to compete. Can Ash and the gang put a smile on its face?

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Ash is having a hard time getting his Chikorita to listen to him, and Nurse Joy thinks that it’s due to a bout of jealousy over Ash’s friendship with Pikachu! Can Ash prove how much Chikorita means to him?

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Our heroes meet a Trainer whose Hoppip partners can predict the weather. But on the horizon, there are two storms approaching...and one of them goes by the name 'Team Rocket'!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: The legendary superhero Gligarman grows old and is beginning to consider hanging up his cape. When Ash and friends wander into town, they bring with them a challenge that could be his final fight: Team Rocket!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: When an unruly herd of Mareep ruin their lunch plans, our heroes decide to help their timid herder in time to compete in a local battle festival.

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: When the top student at a Pokémon training center loses interest in Pokémon battles, opting to build a comprehensive Pokémon database instead, it’s up to Ash to convince him that battling is not just about probability!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: When Ash finds a Cyndaquil in the wild, he is eager to catch it...but you can bet he’s not the only one!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Arriving in Azalea Town, Ash carelessly provokes its residents, who adore SlowPoké. And things only look worse as Team Rocket arrives, meaning to snag every last SlowPoké! What can Ash do?

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: As he promised Professor Oak, Ash delivers the mysterious GS Ball to a renowned Poké Ball expert. But the fruits of his labor are almost spoiled when Team Rocket bursts onto the scene!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Ash has finally reached the Azalea Gym, but Cyndaquil isn’t quite fired up enough to take on the Gym Leader. As the battle heats up, Ash has to give his all to take on the powerful Bug-type Pokémon!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: An inexperienced young Trainer has managed to scare off his own Farfetch’d. Our heroes are happy to help him out, but they'll have to find it before Team Rocket does!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: At an annual Pokémon trading festival, a young Trainer is looking for a good trade for his Wobbuffet. The Team Rocket trio are looking for opportunities as well, but both sets of Trainers get more than they bargain for!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: An unexpected Pokémon firefighting competition reunites our heroes with some old friends, the Squirtle Squad! But they are in desperate need of some leadership, so Ash and Squirtle rejoin the ranks.

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: When they stumble upon a preschool for Wooper and an emergency calls the teacher away, our heroes come to the rescue! But, as always, Team Rocket is around the corner and up to no good…

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: On the way to Goldenrod City, our heroes are forced to head through a tunnel guarded by angry Onix. To make it more difficult, Team Rocket, Snubbull, and Jigglypuff drop in for a visit…

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Following a string of mysterious thefts, our heroes discover some Houndour struggling to survive in the wild. To help these thieving Pokémon mend their ways, Ash and friends will first have to earn their trust.

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Ash and Misty’s rivalry is reignited when they both try to capture a Totodile at the same time. Unable to see whose Poké Ball actually caught it, they decide a battle is the best way to decide Totodile’s new Trainer.

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: When Ash hears of a virtually unbeatable Trainer nearby, he's itching to take on the challenge. But both Brock’s Vulpix and Ash’s Cyndaquil take a beating from her Skarmory, even with their type advantage! How can Ash defeat it?

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Ash’s Totodile develops a crush on a passing Azumarill, the star performer of a Pokémon circus. When Team Rocket arrives to steal the show, Azumarill’s heart isn't the only thing Totodile has to win!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: Ash has his sights set on a rare Noctowl with unusual coloring, but this clever Pokémon repeatedly uses Hypnosis to get the better of him! Will Ash be able to catch this Noctowl napping?

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: A surprise attack from a group of Ursaring has our heroes and Team Rocket running for cover. In the chaos, the groups are split up, and the unenthusiastic pairings are forced to work together. But for how long?

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: After seeing a sign in the forest warning them about mischievous Ghost-type Pokémon, our heroes reach a town whose residents use Psychic types to repel them. But soon some spooky news arrives: a giant Gengar is on the loose!

programme.title: Pokemon: The Johto Journeys
programme.desc: A sudden craze in Pokémon fortune-telling turns out to be a plot to steal Pokémon from local Trainers. But Jessie, James and Meowth aren't behind it this time! Who are these con artists?

programme.title: Pokemon: Johto League Champions
programme.desc: Arriving in Goldenrod City, Ash is ready for his next Gym battle, but the Gym appears to be closed. Their plans delayed, our heroes decide to explore the vibrant city.

programme.title: Pokemon: Johto League Champions
programme.desc: After losing to Whitney at the Goldenrod Gym, Ash is thinking of a rematch with her powerful Miltank. When Whitney suggests a trip to a Miltank dairy, it’s just the chance he's is looking for!

programme.title: Pokemon: Johto League Champions
programme.desc: With his new Gym badge in hand, Ash is asked to appear on a local radio station. But while he's acting out a radio drama with some 'professional actors,' the scripted danger becomes all too lifelike!

programme.title: Pokemon: Johto League Champions
programme.desc: A Bug Pokémon Catching Contest turns into a hive of activity when Ash goes up against his rival Casey—and it isn’t long before the Team Rocket villains swarm the scene, intent on catching a few things for themselves…

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Joeys Choice
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera face "double trouble" when the same guy unknowingly asks them both out on the same date!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: A Tall Tale
programme.desc: Prom night could wind up being a date with disaster when Tia and Tamera's escorts behave badly, and Roger's date towers over him.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Its a Love Thang
programme.desc: Love is in the air when Tamera, Lisa, and Ray all have close encounters of the romantic kind.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Free Billy
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera rescue a pig who's being mistreated by its owner and attempt to secretly stash the "porker" at home.

programme.title: Sister, Sister
programme.desc: While recovering from "twin appendectomies," Tia and Tamera go in search of hospital records that could reveal the identities of their biological parents.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Tattoo
programme.desc: When Lisa forbids Tia to see her boyfriend Michael, Tamera acts as a go-between for the couple -- but finds herself falling for him.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Two for the Road
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera take their sisterly bond a little too far when they collide with each other's cars while learning to drive.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Its A Party Thang
programme.desc: When the "cool crowd" doesn't invite Tia and Tamera to their "happening" party, the twins decide to throw a bash of their own -- which could wind up being a "bust"!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Field Trip
programme.desc: A class field trip to a museum lands the twins in detention, while Roger "borrows" a statue that makes him irresistible to women.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Put to the Test
programme.desc: When Tia and Tamera's SAT scores come in, A-student Tia is stunned to discover that Tamera has pulled a higher score than she has.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Kid in Play
programme.desc: After Tamera wins the lead in the school play, she transforms from a calm high school student into the ultimate "drama diva," who's driving everyone -- particularly Tia -- crazy.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Dream Lover
programme.desc: When Tia confides in Tamera that she dreamed of kissing "Stinky Steve," their boss at Rocket Burger, Steve mistakenly believes she's got "the hots" for him.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Scrambled Eggs
programme.desc: A school project in which Tia and Tamera "marry" Michael and Roger proves to be an eye-opening experience for the twins.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Smoking in the Girls Room
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera have a new "burning passion" -- for cigarettes.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Playing Hooky
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera are caught in the middle when Ray and Lisa square off over Lisa's decision to let the twins "play hooky" from school

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Single White Teenager
programme.desc: When Tia's out sick from school, Tamera befriends a new girl who becomes obsessed with looking and acting like the twins.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: I Do?
programme.desc: Lisa gets "cold feet" after Terrence pops the question, while the twins worry that Lisa's marriage means they'll stop living under the same roof.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Natural
programme.desc: Tia rearranges her class schedule in order to spend more time with the school "stud"; Tamera is tapped to pitch a big softball game

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Break-Up
programme.desc: Lisa decides it's time to "dump" Terrence and forbids Ray from hanging out with him, while Roger tries to pick which twin to "woo."

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Tutor
programme.desc: When a high school football star needs a tutor, Tamera pretends to be Tia so she can teach him a special lesson in "chemistry"!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: History A La Cart
programme.desc: An embarrassing arrest from his college days comes back to haunt Ray, and Lisa turns an inheritance into a shopping spree

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Grandpa Campbell
programme.desc: When Ray's ne'er-do-well father comes for a visit, he turns the household upside-down -- and turns Tamera into a ruthless businesswoman!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Twins Get Fired
programme.desc: When Tia becomes Tamera's new boss at Rocket Burger, they both learn a lesson in responsibility

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Halloween
programme.desc: While the twins go in search of a Halloween rave, Lisa and Ray attend a fancy party thrown by a prospective client.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Weird Science
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera think they're in for a "free ride" when Ray starts dating their science teacher.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Thanksgiving in Hawaii (Part I)
programme.desc: When Ray and Lisa take the girls to Hawaii for Thanksgiving, they're forced to share their condo with their "exes," who have begun dating each other.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Thanksgiving in Hawaii (Part II)
programme.desc: While Ray and Lisa frantically search for the twins, who are missing in Hawaii, Roger opens the Campbell/Landry home to hobos for Thanksgiving dinner.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Private School
programme.desc: The prospect of Tia's attending a prestigious prep school puts social pressure on Tia and financial pressure on Lisa.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Christmas
programme.desc: When a loan shark comes after Ray's father for a bad debt, the family comes to his rescue.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Double Double Date
programme.desc: The twins find themselves in double trouble when they switch identities and Tia winds up with two dates for the same party.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Reality Really Bites
programme.desc: For her film class, Tia "spices up" her family documentary by throwing all sorts of monkey wrenches into their lives.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Volunteers
programme.desc: While volunteering at a local retirement home, Tamera becomes determined to help a grumpy old man find happiness again.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Valentines Day
programme.desc: Valentine's Day finds Tamera trying to "steal back" her hot date, and Ray and Lisa wondering if the other person is in love with them.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Paper or Plastic?
programme.desc: Tamera crosses the picket lines to work at a supermarket, but soon realizes that being a "scab" isn't all it's cracked up to be.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Piano Lesson
programme.desc: Ray learns a "noteworthy" lesson in honesty when he "fibs" and tells a tone-deaf Tamera that she plays the piano well, and she winds up humiliating herself at a piano recital.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Summer Bummer
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera trade places so that Tia can be with a cute guy in summer school and Tamera can "goof off" by taking Tia's spot as a camp counselor.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Candidate
programme.desc: Tamera learns how much she needs Tia's help when she runs for class president.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Big Twin on Campus
programme.desc: When Tia poses as a college student to impress her older boyfriend, Tamera goes to great lengths to fit in with Tia's new circle of "mature" friends.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One discovers that they need to take new security measures to protect the system they use to follow crimes after they are given acces to a crime about to happen.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One's efforts to save a kidnapped woman's life are dependent in large part on the abductor's brother, who holds a dark family secret.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One confronts a man desperate to keep the promise he made to his deaf nephew by protecting the only place the boy has ever felt safe.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: The team is called in when a car bomb explodes at a kid's birthday party. As it turns out the victim of the explosion was the head of a local biker community which is already under investigation.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: The team tries to help a young man with amazing memory who is taken hostage to help get to a weapon with smart bullets which can shoot a target even behind cover.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Jules and Sam receive some personal news, but don't know whether to share it with the team.
programme.sub-title: We Take Care of Our Own

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Ed and Greg's sons come for a ride along and disobey a direct order from Sergeant Parker when he tells them to remain in the truck while Team One deals with an incident between three cops on an off-the-books mission to clean up the streets.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One is called in when an Emotionally Disturbed Person kidnaps his neighbor's baby, thinking it's his own daughter, and takes refuge on a ferry.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One faces the most challenging call of their careers when a series of bombs tears the city apart.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One continues the hunt for a bomber at large as team members, joined by EMS and the military, split up to deal with multiple bomb threats in a city already plunged into chaos.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: When a disturbed man takes a woman hostage outside an office building, the SRU is quick to respond to save the innocent hostage and bystanders caught in the crossfire.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: After a desperate father puts his own life on the line to save his daughter's life, Sgt. Parker is forced to confront his feelings about his own child

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: When the SRU team raids a drug dealer, things quickly go south as an innocent bystander finds himself in the middle of a gun fight.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: The SRU team must set aside their personal beliefs when they go to save a cop who is being held hostage by an enraged family member.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: When the SRU arrives at a hostage situation at a bank, Sgt. Parker faces one of his toughest negotiations when the robber isn't who he thought.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Teenage gang members are out for revenge after a distressed classmate accuses one of their boyfriends of rape.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: When the SRU responds to a violent altercation between a young man and his father, the boy flees the police and runs into the woods, sending the team on a manhunt

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: When a young man holds a courtroom hostage in an attempt to clear his name for a crime he didn't commit, an SRU team member is in the wrong place at the wrong time and must find a way to safely defuse a critical situation.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: What starts out as a routine security watch for the SRU turns into a critical situation when the wife of the man they were hired to protect is kidnapped from underneath their noses.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: When a woman kidnaps another woman whom she suspects of having an affair with her husband, the SRU is called to the scene.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Today in Teletubbyland, the Teletubbies have special things to do. Andy Brown and some children drive through a safari park to see some monkeys.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children who are on a drive around a Game Park in South Africa. A trolley appears in Teletubbyland. Po wanted Tinky Winky to pull her in the trolley, but Tinky Winky was feeling tired.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Dipsy fetches the others to watch Andy Brown and some children looking at Peacocks. The magical tree appears in Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies dance a figure of 8 round the hills and then watch as some children visit a pier. The children look at the sea and watch some men fishing from the end of the pier.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet rises and the Teletubbies run out of the house to listen to the Woof Woof sounds. The Teletubbies copy the sounds again and again.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po invites Tinky Winky to dance, and they dance all around Home Hill. Tamzin Griffin pretends to be Funny Lady and tells the story of the very proud crown.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Andy Brown and some children feed some birds and watch them playing on the bird table. In Teletubbyland, it's timer for Tubby Custard, but Dipsy spills his all over a Tubby Seat. No-one wants to sit in the Tubby Custard, so Noo-Noo tidies it up.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch a little boy show us how to make a Turban. The Tubbies go for a walk, but Po, Laa-Laa and Dipsy want to play with their favourite things, so Tinky Winky goes for a walk on his own – all the way home.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky says "Eh-oh". The Teletubbies and some children watch Tamzin Griffin pretend to be the Funny Lady and tell the story of the naughty hat.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Tubbies watch two boys having their hair cut. A mirror appears in Teletubbyland. Laa-Laa, Dipsy and Tinky Winky liked looking at their reflection in the mirror.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po brings the Tubbies out of the house to watch Tamzin Griffin pretend to be Funny Lady and tell the story of the silly soppy soap. It's time for Po to have a wash with her Tubby Sponge and Noo Noo helps her.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Andy Brown and some children visit and aquarium to learn how things swim in the water. The Teletubbies play an in and out game when a cloud appears in Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children going for a walk to look for dandelion clocks to blow. Po decides to have a blowy day and has fun blowing at everything in Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch Andy Brown and some children visit some cygnets. In Teletubbyland, it's time for the Tubbies to go to bed, but Po is not tired, so she rides her scooter.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Laa-Laa run into Home Hill to say 'Eh-oh', but where 's Po? Some children help their dad to wash his car. They get very wet and soapy.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies dance in and out of the trees. The Teletubbies watch Matthew, James and Mark feeding their chickens. There's toast all over Teletubbyland when the Tubby Toaster makes too much toast.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Some children play a game called oranges and lemons. A bell appears in Teletubbyland and makes a lovely ringing noise. The noise is so loud it makes the Tubbies fall over laughing

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies run across the hills to say "Eh-oh" and watch some children playing tennis. Laa-Laa and Po love to play with the ball. They roll, throw and kick it to each other.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Laa-Laa says "Eh-oh" and then runs off to join the others watch some children learn about the number ten. The Teletubbies love to play games.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies find each other for a big hug before watching a little girl read her sister a story that she has written and illustrated herself. It’s called ‘The Girl In The Back Garden’.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch a little girl make some Bagels with her uncle. It’s time for Tubby Custard. Laa-Laa sits down to eat her Tubby Custard, but she waits for Po.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies run around the house before watching some children paint in circles. Tinky Winky and Dipsy make some Tubby Custard, The Noo-noo tidies up all of Dipsy’s Tubby Custard, so Dipsy and Tinky Winky chase the Noo-noo.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Tubbies watch a little boy talk about his cat. Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Laa‑Laa say "Eh-oh" to Po through the window.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies love to eat Tubby Custard, but Po spills hers on the floor and the Noo-Noo cleans up the mess.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet rises to teach Dipsy a nursery rhyme. The Teletubbies watch two children playing with water. It rains in Teletubbyland, and makes a puddle.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Some girls live with their Mum and Dad on a boat. They show us inside the boat. The Teletubbies come out of the house. Tinky Winky and Po go left and Dipsy and Laa-Laa go right.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch a little boy called Brennan pretend to fly to the moon. In Teletubbyland, the Teletubbies are going out to play.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch how some children who live on an island go to school by boat. A voice trumpet appears in Teletubbyland and the Teletubbies play a game called here and there.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies play a sitting and standing game. They watch some children who are sitting and standing too. Po is riding on her scooter. She hasn’t gone very far when she finds Dipsy’s hat.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch two girls climb a hill and look at the view. In Teletubbyland, Po looks for Tinky Winky. Over the hills, between the trees and along the path, but she couldn’t see him.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Tubbies laugh when laugh when they bump into each other and fall over. They all watch two girls meet Tito a Flamenco guitar player who plays them a nursery rhyme. A guitar appears in Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch a girl talking all about her Dad's lorry. Dipsy goes for a long walk, but the other Teletubbies decide not to join him.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: It's time for Dipsy to have some Tubby Toast. The Teletubbies watch some children have fun in their paddling pool on a sunny day.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Dipsy has no time for Tubby Custard, before watching a little girl help feed her baby sister. Po makes some Tubby Custard for Dipsy, but every time she comes to the table, she forgets and eats it all herself.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet rises to play some double bass music for Laa-Laa. The Tubbies watch some children visit their friend who plays the double bass.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po fetches the other to watch Andy Brown and two children look for some rabbits. In Teletubbyland, Tinky Winky is looking for Po.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The toaster makes an extra piece of Tubby Toast – but who is going to eat it? The Teletubbies watch Emily And Jamie take their dog Jessie for a walk before giving her a bath.

programme.title: Skyscrapers and Car Parks
programme.desc: Join Bob on his next visit On Site! Follow Bob and the team as they take us through the construction of skyscrapers & car parks. Learn how skyscrapers are built up high on strong foundations and see how lifts are needed to carry people to the top.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: The whole town is practicing for an egg and spoon race, which Mrs Percival has organised. Trix decides to enter, but unfortunately she practices with fresh eggs and in her usual haste she manages to make a great mess in JJ’s yard.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is building a new exercise park which Mr Dixon’s brother David, a famous goalkeeper, is going to open. Dizzy tries to cajole Spud into joining in a game of soccer, but uncharacteristically the scarecrow doesn’t want to play.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Train enthusiast Mr Bentley is away for the day and, in his absence, Mrs Bentley decides to surprise her husband by asking Bob to build him a brand new model railway in the shed.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: After a stormy night, Wendy has to mend a chimney pot at Mrs Potts’, while Bob repairs a drainage pipe for Farmer Pickles. A wire fence has been blown away at the farm too, but fixing the pipe takes priority.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is building a goat run for Farmer Pickles new goat – Giddy. It’s Travis’s job to collect Giddy from Meg MacDonald the goat breeder. Spud teases Travis about being slow and Travis resolves to collect the goat very quickly.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: An observation tower is going to be added next to the Visitor’s centre to provide a scenic view of Sunflower Valley. The scaffolding is raised and Roley prepares a path to connect the tower to the Dome.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is replacing a potting shed on Mr Beasley’s allotment. In the old shed is a chest which Mr Beasley doesn’t need any more. Meanwhile Wendy calls at JJ’s for the materials for the new shed.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Scoop has spent the night in Bobsville and is off back to Sunflower Valley early in the morning. Benny is desperate to go too so Scoop promises to come back and take him as soon as he can.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: The Team are converting Mr Sabatini’s windmill into a café. Roley is rolling the ground flat for all the tables and chairs while Bob makes the awnings. Roley hits trouble when molehills keep appearing where he’s just been rolling.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and the gang have been asked to replace some burst pipes under the main road. Meanwhile Jenny is in town to persuade Wendy to take a much-needed holiday. Unfortunately, there’s lots of work to do and Wendy simply doesn’t have time to go away.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is building a marshland walkway with his Dad’s help. Benny hears that the marsh is ‘like a jungle’ and becomes determined to finds some jungle animals like tigers. Every time he spots one, however, it turns out to be something very ordinary.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob’s built a new waterwheel for the old watermill. He’s made it at the yard, but now needs to dismantle it to take it to the mill in sections. Bob needs Benny, Scoop, Lofty and Muck to carry the wheel sections through the marshland to the mill.

programme.title: Knights of Can-A-Lot
programme.desc: Bob, Wendy and the team get a big surprise when Bob's father Robert comes to visit. When stern Ms Chalmers from the National Heritage asks Bob to renovate the old castle a case of mistaken identity leaves Bob’s father in charge!

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: The team are building Sunflower Valley’s first geodesic dome. As Wendy has organised the dome and knows how it all fits together, Bob suggests that she might like to take charge of the job. Reluctantly Wendy agrees.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and the team are building a special flat-packed eco-home for Marjorie. When Muck and Benny spot Marjorie’s canvas tent and Spud’s straw-house, they wonder what else houses can be made from.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and the gang are replacing some old pipes outside the town hall. Molly, meanwhile, is practicing for her first aid exam with a reluctant JJ as guinea pig. When Wendy calls JJ’s yard to order a special pipe and Molly offers to deliver it.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob’s renovating a dilapidated old watermill in Sunflower Valley – it’s going to make flour from Farmer Pickles sunflower farm. After checking that it’s safe, Bob realises that one of the millstones has gone missing. How very odd!

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Farmer Pickles has a new family of Pigs being delivered to his farm and Bob must build a new home for them. In the meantime, Farmer Pickles places the pigs in an old Barn to keep them safe.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: It’s a big day. The team are building Farmer Pickles new factory which will turn sunflowers into sunflower oil. Everyone is excited to meet Marjorie, who arrives by bicycle. Marjorie has been brought on board by Farmer Pickles to manage the factory.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Robert’s come to the valley to spend some quality time with Bob. They have a plan to spend the afternoon birdwatching – Robert wants to see the very rare lesser-striped sunflower warbler.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is building a pavilion in the park to celebrate the annual flower show. Molly has built a special statue of a horse for the occasion which is to be unveiled at the opening ceremony.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: It’s a blistering hot day on Sunflower Valley. Bob and Wendy have got to finish building their workshop – making the workbenches and installing the power tools – it’s an inside job so the machines all get the day off.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Mr and Mrs Sabatini have asked Bob to convert a local house into a delicatessen. Unfortunately for Roley, there is no rolling to be done. The gang leaves to get on with the job and Roley decides to tag along just in case.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is tiling a roof and calls at JJ’s yard to pick up the materials. But there is only one pallet of the requisite red tiles in stock, so JJ promises to send the remainder of the order as soon as his delivery arrives.

programme.title: Trains and Treehouses
programme.desc: Bob and his ‘Can-Do’ crew are back on site with a brand new adventure! Join Bob as he builds a train set for Mr Bentley as a birthday surprise and helps find a family of squirrels a new tree-house home.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Trix is looking for something to do so JJ sends her to deliver a bag of wood chippings to Bob who is building a Jungle Jim climbing frame in the school playground. Trix likes being busy and starts offering to fetch and carry everything for everyone.

programme.title: When Bob Became a Builder
programme.desc: It’s a beautiful day in Sunflower Valley and Bob and his machine team are building a new picnic area at the beach. Dizzy, Lofty and Scrambler are clearing stuff that’s been washed up on the beach, while Benny has a great time digging with Scoop and Muck.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Its early morning and there’s a strange creature in Sunflower Valley. First of all it visits the Homestead and then Scarecrow Cottage scaring Sumsy and Travis. It’s something big and hairy, but gets frightened away by Scrambler carrying Baz.

programme.title: Roads and Bridges
programme.desc: For the first time ever, Bob is going On Site! Join Bob and his Can-Do crew as they explore real life construction sites. See diggers, mixers and trucks work together to build roads and bridges.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is installing an antique fireplace for Mrs Percival. He goes to JJ’s yard to collect it and finds JJ playing with his new model aeroplane. Bob then heads to Mrs Percival’s and starts to knock out the old fireplace.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: It is Wendy’s birthday, but she insists she doesn’t want any fuss – such as a surprise party. She heads off to start the day’s work, laying a Heritage Trail around the town, and Bob reveals to the Machines that he is not planning a party.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Farmer Pickles is having problems with mice eating his barley seed so he asks Bob to build a seed store to keep it safe. Whilst Bob is working, Farmer Pickles decides to go and buy some replacement seed.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is building a Seaweed Farm for Annie Pickles – Farmer Pickles cousin. Scrambler volunteers to help Annie collect seaweed seedling on the beach.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Scoop and Dizzy have spent the night at Bob’s old yard in Bobsville to help Wendy with some digging and mixing. The machines all wish that Wendy could come and live out in Sunflower Valley.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and the team are drilling a special well at their homestead. The well has its own water tank, which needs to be raised from the ground on rocks. Wendy and Scrambler arrive for the first ever time in Sunflower Valley..

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and the gang are building the road connecting Bob’s mobile home site with the location of the new yard. It’s a big job for Roley, but just as Bob’s explaining the job, a pretty little bird flies overhead clutching some twigs in her beak.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob has a plan to accommodate all of the newcomers. He’s designed a kit house that clips together quickly and easily. Bob’s dad Robert comes to the valley to help build the houses, while Mr and Mrs Bentley conduct a survey on the newcomers’ requirements.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: It’s a big day, as the team are building Bob’s new workshop at his new yard. Scoop is desperate to get stuck in and beavers about doing everything he can to help. Bob needs some old floorboards collecting from Bobsville, and sends Scoop to pick them up.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob’s just finished tidying his mobile home when Farmer Pickles comes and asks if he’ll help with his Sunflower Harvest. He needs a silo built in which to store the ripe sunflowers before they’re taken to the factory.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Wendy and Bob have a very special announcement to make. They are going to fix up an old Houseboat for Wendy to live in. There’s a lot of hard work to be done. They need to make a gang plank, fix solar panels and give it a really good clean.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is putting up some new signs in Sunflower Valley to help people find their way round the valley. Meanwhile Dizzy has been asked by Ela Stevenson if she’ll be the ‘walking bus’ and help the children get to school.

programme.title: The Legend of the Golden Hammer
programme.desc: Our first sight of Fixham is in the episodic special The Legend Of The Golden Hammer. The Legend of the Golden Hammer consists of six stories in which Bob and his team tackle building jobs at six of Fixham’s key locations.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob’s too busy to even tell the machines what the job is for today. He takes Scrambler to the dome and the others follow. It turns out that they are going to turn the whole of the Dome into a Visitors centre and its opening tomorrow!

programme.title: Mega Machines: The Movie
programme.desc: Scoop, Muck and Lofty can’t wait to help Bob with his biggest build ever – building a dam and transforming an old quarry into a reservoir to provide Spring City with fresh water.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Travis is delighted to receive his own Talkie Talkie and can’t wait to make good use of it. Mr Beasley arrives in the valley with a load of yoghurt pots as he heard something on the radio about making them into a house.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and the gang are building a pedestrian crossing. Mrs Percival, meanwhile, has asked Molly to paint a mural of a jungle scene in the school playground.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: The team are taking a break from building so they can tidy-up all the left over materials from around the Valley. Farmer Pickles asks Bob if he’ll put together Scruffty’s new kennel. Bob’s got a busy morning but agrees to build the kennel later on.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is building an extraordinary new cave-house for Sandy Beach – a caveman expert who’s moving to the valley to study how cavemen used to live. The team are all head into the mountains.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Mr Ellis has a batch of Doves delivered, to use in his magic act, and asks Bob to build a dovecote for his new birds to live in. Mr Ellis realises that he has to go to work but does not want to leave the birds at home alone.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: A new machine has come to the Valley – Packer the Delivery Truck. He’s to work with Farmer Pickles team. Farmer Pickles gives Packer his jobs and he sets off on his first delivery round.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Mr Ellis needs Bob to replace an old rotten beam in his cottage. The gang set off for Mr Ellis’s and Pilchard sneaks along – she knows that cottages are popular with mice! At the cottage, Mr Ellis reports some strange goings-on at the cottage.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Russell Murders
programme.desc: Bludgeoned to death whilst walking down a country lane, the horrific murders of Lin and Megan Russell made worldwide news. Discover the events of that shocking day.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Peter Sutcliffe
programme.desc: Arguably Britain's most infamous serial killer, Peter Sutcliffe became known as The Yorkshire Ripper when he began murdering women in Yorkshire during the 1970s.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Colin Ireland
programme.desc: In the summer of 1993, gay men in London were being terrorised by a brutal killer. Colin Ireland, a loner from Southend, had made a macabre resolution – to become a serial killer.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Jimmy Savile
programme.desc: Jimmy Savile: From national treasure to one of the UK's most notorious sex offenders: this is the story of how the revelation of Jimmy Savile's crimes left the UK reeling.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Raoul Moat
programme.desc: The hunt for Raoul Moat was one of the largest in British history, after he wounded his ex-girlfriend, killed her new partner and shot and blinded police constable David Rathband.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Shipman
programme.desc: Doctor Harold Shipman was convicted of killing 15 patients in 2000, but he is thought to have killed up to 215. Discover how he was eventually caught by detectives.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Sarah Payne
programme.desc: Eight-year-old Sarah Payne's disappearance and murder shocked the nation. Here, Sarah's mum reveals her tireless campaign to protect other children from paedophilia.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Philpotts
programme.desc: When six children died in a house fire in Allenton, Derby in May 2012 it shocked the nation. Parents Mick and Mairead Philpott were charged with the murders. We uncover the reasons behind the arson attack on their own home.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Lee Rigby
programme.desc: In broad daylight on a busy Woolwich street on 22nd May 2013, serving soldier Fusilier Lee Rigby was attacked and murdered by Islamic extremists Michael Adebolajo and Michael Adebowale.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Stephen Lawrence
programme.desc: On the 20th anniversary of his death, the family of Stephen Lawrence reflect on his tragic murder in 1993, as well as their lengthy fight for justice.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Dunblane Massacre
programme.desc: In an emotional tribute, witnesses and victims' relatives look back on the tragic murder of 16 children and a teacher at Dunblane Primary School in Scotland, 15 years ago.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Rose West
programme.desc: With her husband Fred, Rose West brutally murdered ten girls between 1973 and 1979, making her one of the most high profile serial killers in the UK.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Shipman
programme.desc: Doctor Harold Shipman was convicted of killing 15 patients in 2000, but he is thought to have killed up to 215. Discover how he was eventually caught by detectives.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Peter Tobin
programme.desc: Peter Tobin is one of the most notorious serial killers in the UK. From 1991–2006 he raped and murdered three young girls; Dinah McNicol, Vicky Hamilton and Angelika Kluk. Yet even prior to these horrific murders he had a history of violence.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Gemma McCluskie
programme.desc: The harrowing story of EastEnders actress Gemma McCluskie, who was murdered by her older brother in the flat they shared in east London in 2012.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Suzy Lamplugh
programme.desc: A look at the case of estate agent Suzy Lamplugh, who showed a client around a house in Fulham in July 1986 and was never seen again. Dermot Murnaghan hosts.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Joanne Dennehy
programme.desc: Dermot Murnaghan returns to uncover the truth behind the horrific crimes of Peterborough serial killer Joanna Dennehy.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Murder Of Jill Dando
programme.desc: Documentary examining the unsolved murder of British TV presenter and newsreader Jill Dando, who was shot outside her house in Fulham, London, in 1999.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Rochdale Groomers
programme.desc: Rochdale Groomers: Documentary examining the sex abuse scandal which occurred in the North of England and appeared to go undetected for quite some time.

programme.desc: The beginning of Toni’s life with Todd could spell the end of her friendship with Joan.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Who's Your Daddy?

programme.title: Girlfriends: Porn to Write

programme.title: Girlfriends: All the Creatures Were Stirring
programme.desc: Joan and William are stranded by a blizzard.

programme.title: Girlfriends: P.D.A. – D.O.A
programme.desc: William is jealous of Joan’s former fiancé.

programme.title: Count Duckula: No Yaks Please, We're Tibetan
programme.desc: The trio travel to Tibet

programme.title: Sabrina The Animated Series: Shrink to Fit
programme.desc: Sabrina and Chloe try to compete with Gem by fitting into the latest fad jeans.

programme.title: Sabrina The Animated Series: Nothin' Says Lovin' Like Something From a Coven
programme.desc: After being dissed from Gem's Halloween party, Sabrina throws a Halloween party of her own.
programme.sub-title: Nothin' Says Lovin' Like Something From a Coven

programme.title: Sabrina The Animated Series: Wag the Witch
programme.desc: Sabrina competes with Gem for office of Class President. They play dirty until Gem accuses her of being weird. Sabrina goes to disastrous lengths to prove her wrong.

programme.title: Sabrina The Animated Series: Witchy Grrrls
programme.desc: Sabrina's band, the Flavor Babes, use a little magic to launch their careers. The much desired fame and riches end up tearing Sabrina, Chloe, Hilda and Zelda apart.

programme.title: Sabrina The Animated Series: Paranormal Pi
programme.desc: When Pi sees Sabrina's Uncle Zamboni flying through the air he is convinced there are paranormal rumblings in Greendale. He makes it his mission to prove it to the townspeople.

programme.title: Sabrina The Animated Series: Anywhere But Here
programme.desc: Sabrina is tired of the restrictions that come with being a kid. She casts a spell that catapults into adulthood.

programme.title: Sabrina The Animated Series: Documagicary
programme.desc: Sabrina ends up in a new docu-series called, That Witch which becomes an overnight sensation! Sabrina soon tires of the demanding schedule and lack of privacy.

programme.title: Sabrina The Animated Series: The Grandparent Trap
programme.desc: When Sabrina hears that her Grandparents are coming for visit she is excited. They have a important announcement to make.

programme.title: Sabrina The Animated Series: I Got Glue Babe
programme.desc: Everyone is fed up with Sabrina and Gem's constant rivalry. Sabrina casts a spell to make Gem and herself closer. But she uses too much potion and it causes them to stick together.

programme.title: Tales from the Cryptkeeper: Transylvania Express
programme.desc: When surfer dudes Ben and Mike mistakenly travel to Austria instead of Australia, they find themselves aboard the midnight run of the Translyvania Express railway.

programme.title: Tales from the Cryptkeeper: Cold Blood Warm Hearts / The Spider and the Files
programme.desc: Kristin drives out to a small fishing community to investigate a local sighting of a sea monster. / Sixteen year old Simon's fascination with spiders becomes a nightmare when his turn of the century town is infested with arachnids of every variety.
programme.sub-title: Cold Blood Warm Hearts / The Spider and the Files

programme.title: Tales from the Cryptkeeper: Sharon Sharalike
programme.desc: Selfish eight year old Shauna doesn't like to share, especially with her five year old sister Katie.

programme.title: Tales from the Cryptkeeper: Too Cool for School
programme.desc: Junior High School friends Stephanie and Jan hate rules.

programme.title: Tales from the Cryptkeeper: Waste Not, Haunt Not
programme.desc: Richard and his younger brother Stephen, are willing to try anything to design the winning Science Fair project.

programme.title: Tales from the Cryptkeeper: Trouble in Store
programme.desc: Best friends Derek and Rob love new things.

programme.title: Tales from the Cryptkeeper: All Booked Up
programme.desc: Thirteen year old Greg Davis hates books.

programme.title: Tales from the Cryptkeeper: So Very Attractive

programme.title: Tales from the Cryptkeeper: Drawn and Quartered
programme.desc: Aspiring artist Ralph Philips is focused on perfecting his art.

programme.title: Tales from the Cryptkeeper: Imaginary Friend

programme.title: Tales from the Cryptkeeper: Town Gathering
programme.desc: Fourteen year old Erin has little else to do in her small town than to make up stories.

programme.title: Bitten - Starts at 9PM!
programme.desc: In order to end a decades-old vendetta, Elena Michaels leads her family into a vicious war against the Russian Alpha and his Pack, who will stop at nothing to wipe out her bloodline.

programme.title: Bitten - Starts at 9PM!
programme.desc: In order to end a decades-old vendetta, Elena Michaels leads her family into a vicious war against the Russian Alpha and his Pack, who will stop at nothing to wipe out her bloodline.

programme.title: Bitten: Right Behind You
programme.desc: More of Roman’s soldiers descend on Bear Valley as Elena struggles to convince Jeremy to protect her blood family against the Russian Alpha, and his quest to kill Sasha.

programme.title: Bitten: A Quiet Dog
programme.desc: Jeremy attempts to broker a negotiation to end the thirty-year blood feud between Sasha and Roman. Meanwhile, Elena watches over Pack prisoner, Konstantin, who tries to deduce why she is protecting Sasha.

programme.title: Bitten: Of Sonders Weight
programme.desc: Jeremy must deal with the fallout of the failed negotiations between Roman and Sasha; first and foremost, how much did Karen Morgan see? As Elena tends to a convalescing Sasha, he tells her the truth about why he abandoned her thirty years ago.

programme.title: Bitten: Rule Of Anger
programme.desc: Elena and Clay face off against a deadly new foe in order to save the imprisoned Marsten. Meanwhile, Paige and Nick work together to figure out what is causing the magical outbreaks from Rachel’s baby, Rocco.

programme.title: Bitten: On The Brink
programme.desc: Nick, Paige and Rachel use magic to track Katia, who has kidnaped baby Rocco. Elena invites Konstantin to Stonehaven, asking for his help in ending the blood feud and calling off the Russian assassin.

programme.title: Bitten: Tili Tili Bom
programme.desc: As newly appointed Alpha, Elena’s first goal is to take out the Albino. But when she and Clay confront the Russian assassin, they quickly discover he is always two steps ahead.

programme.title: Bitten: Shock The System
programme.desc: As the Pack’s new Alpha, Elena rallies the troops, but when she tries to bolster a grieving Sasha, they are lured into the woods and taken prisoner by the Albino. Jeremy wages a psychological war against his captor, Roman Navikev.

programme.title: Bitten: Truth, Changes, Everything
programme.desc: Elena comes up with a bold and dangerous plan to take back Stonehaven and save what remains of the Pack. If successful, her idea will do much more than save her family and their home – what she plans to do will change everything, forever.

programme.title: Bitten: Summons
programme.desc: After the mauled body of a girl shows up, Elena Michaels, a female werewolf, is forced to choose between her new human life and the more-than-human existence she’s forsaken at Stonehaven

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Stolen Crime
programme.desc: A man demands to be arrested for murdering his wealthy wife whose death is attributed to natural causes. A valuable coin collection holds the answers.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Silver Curtain
programme.desc: Behind the "silver curtain" spray from a fountain, a man is found dead with a knife in his back. Colonel March is called in to investigate.

programme.title: The Grudge 2: The Grudge 2
programme.desc: Acclaimed producers Sam Raimi, Rob Tapert and Taka Ichise have re-teamed with director Takashi Shimizu and screenwriter Stephen Susco to present this heart-stopping sequel to the smash-hit thriller, The Grudge.

programme.title: Open Grave
programme.desc: Waking up in a pit of dead bodies with no memory, a man joins forces with a group of strangers. to figure out who is behind the mysterious mass killings. But suspicions give way to violence, as they discover a much more vicious.

programme.title: The Grudge 3
programme.desc: Jake, the sole survivor of The Grudge 2 massacre, is tortured by chilling visions of Kayako and Toshio that have led to his hospitalization. Jake's caretaker, Dr. Sullivan is determined to investigate his horrifying tales.

programme.title: House of Darkness
programme.desc: Hap (Justin Long) drives Mina (Kate Bosworth) home to her mysterious estate after a night of drinking. Hap gets more than he bargained for when unexpected occurrences offset his attempts at a romantic connection with this mysterious object of his desire.

programme.title: H.G. Wells' The Invisible Man: Behind the Mask
programme.desc: Brady helps a horribly disfigured man to become invisible but is soon caught in lies that threaten to destroy the life of a notable politician.

programme.title: H.G. Wells' The Invisible Man: Shadow on the Screen
programme.desc: Peter must board a vessel to rescue a sailor who wants to defect. However, a trap has been set using radar to detect the Invisible Man on a TV screen.

programme.title: H.G. Wells' The Invisible Man: The Mink Coat
programme.desc: While vacationing, Peter's sister becomes convinced that the inner-lining of an airplane passenger's fur coat is being used for smuggling purposes.

programme.title: H.G. Wells' The Invisible Man: The Locked Room
programme.desc: Peter must infiltrate an embassy to rescue a Russian scientist who wants to defect and has been working to solve the problem of invisibility.

programme.title: H.G. Wells' The Invisible Man: Jail Break
programme.desc: Peter vows to help an innocent men unjustly sentenced to prison for a crime he didn't commit and must find the real thief.

programme.title: H.G. Wells' The Invisible Man: Blind Justice
programme.desc: When an airline pilot is framed for drug smuggling, a killer comes to his home to silence him... and the pilot's blind wife (Honor Blackman) is the only witness.

programme.title: H.G. Wells' The Invisible Man: Bank Raid
programme.desc: Kidnappers abduct Peter's niece and hold her ransom for $50,000. To get her back safely, Dr. Brady is forced to steal the money from a bank vault.

programme.title: H.G. Wells' The Invisible Man: Strange Partners
programme.desc: A wealthy man uses extortion and a trained attack dog to force Peter to kill his partner and make it look like a heart attack.

programme.title: H.G. Wells' The Invisible Man: Odds Against Death
programme.desc: A gangster forces one of Peter's staff to developed a mathematical system to win at the roulette table.

programme.title: H.G. Wells' The Invisible Man: Picnic With Death
programme.desc: Peter's existence as an invisible man becomes public knowledge, while Sally's friend needs help when her stepfather plots to kill her invalid mother.

programme.title: H.G. Wells' The Invisible Man: Play to Kill
programme.desc: A celebrated actress runs down a hobo by accident and a witness blackmails her, threatening to expose her secret.

programme.title: 100 Years of Horror
programme.desc: Men didnt have a lock on the horror movie. There were plenty of frightful females to go around, as youll see in this salute to demonic dames.

programme.title: 100 Years of Horror
programme.desc: Nothing like a good ghost story  except a centurys worth of films filled with more ghost stories!

programme.title: 100 Years of Horror
programme.desc: Lon Chaney Sr. was the original Phantom of the Opera, but there have been other notable portrayals  and other notable phantoms!

programme.title: 100 Years of Horror
programme.desc: Witches have been a favorite of fairy tales  and fright films  for centuries. Heres a wicked look at a covens worth of portrayals.

programme.desc: More than just the Devil, demons have been a mainstay of horror films since the silent days.

programme.title: Knight Rider

programme.title: Creator Classic at Philly Cricket
programme.desc: Watch some of the most prominent golf creators take on the Wissahickon Course at the Philadelphia Cricket Club.

programme.title: Golf Digest
programme.desc: Join Tiger Woods for an in-depth look at how he plays the game.

programme.title: PGA TOUR - The Drop
programme.desc: The Drop blends storytelling, competition, and personality. Each week, the show dives into the intensity of tournament play, previewing what's ahead and recapping the biggest storylines, giving fans a unique look at the players on and off the course.

programme.title: PGA Tour Originals
programme.desc: A look back at Scottie Scheffler's win at the Procore Championship and Team Europe's Ryder Cup win.

programme.title: Scottie Scheffler Wins 2025 Procore Championship
programme.desc: Scottie Scheffler won his sixth event in 2025 and his 19th in his PGA TOUR career in Napa, Calif.

programme.title: 2023 Sanderson Farms Championship - Final Round
programme.desc: The final round of the 2023 Sanderson Farms Championship, where Luke List rose above a five-man playoff field to win.

programme.title: 2021 Sanderson Farms Championship - Final Round
programme.desc: At the 2021 Sanderson Farms Championship, Sam Burns shot a 5-under 67 to claim his second PGA TOUR title.

programme.title: 2022 Sanderson Farms Championship - Final Round
programme.desc: The final round of the 2022 Sanderson Farms Championship, where Mackenzie Hughes outlasted Sepp Straka in a playoff.

programme.title: 2024 Sanderson Farms Championship: Final Round
programme.desc: Kevin Yu outlasts Beau Hossler in a one-hole playoff to win the 2024 Sanderson Farms Championship.

programme.title: 2023 Sanderson Farms Championship - Final Round
programme.desc: The final round of the 2023 Sanderson Farms Championship, where Luke List rose above a five-man playoff field to win.

programme.title: Psych
programme.desc: An impassioned chef from the South, who seeks restitution after his robbery conviction is overturned by The Innocence Project, hires Shawn and Gus to help him find the actual culprit for whom he spent time in jail for.

programme.title: Psych
programme.desc: After over 20 years of searching, the body of Veronica Towne has been found, and Shawn, Gus and Henry reopen the case and revisit all the old suspects, including the son of a millionaire who was dating her at the time she went missing.

programme.title: Psych
programme.desc: After Henry takes a bullet to the chest and is rushed to the hospital, a spiteful Shawn seeks revenge on the shooter and everyone else involved in the scandal.

programme.title: Scorpion: Monkey See, Monkey Poo
programme.desc: To stop a dangerous virus from spreading, Team Scorpion must locate a rare endangered monkey in the Amazon that has the antibody for the cure.

programme.title: Scorpion: Broken Wind
programme.desc: While on a mission to repair a turbine at a wind farm, Team Scorpion must race to save Paige and Happy before the cable holding the gondola they are in snaps and sends them plummeting to the ground.

programme.title: Scorpion: Rock Block
programme.desc: Team Scorpion must defy the orders of a foreign government after they retrieve an asteroid from space containing DNA strands that could have deadly consequences once exposed.

programme.desc:

programme.title: Mr. D: Gerry Goes To Prison
programme.desc: In an effort to make some fast money before March break, Gerry takes a side job teaching in a prison.

programme.title: Mr. D: A Star Is Born
programme.desc: Gerry steps into the role of a lifetime and finds himself bitten by the acting bug and reveling in the newfound attention.

programme.title: Mr. D: Gerry Throws The Game
programme.desc: Gerry takes on the senior girls’ volleyball team. But when he realizes their semifinals will be interfering with his all-important social schedule, he sets out to sabotage them at the quarterfinals.

programme.title: Mr. D: The Man With Two Red Shoes
programme.desc: When Gerry learns of a lurker near the school, he sets out to catch said lurker and earn the Teacher of the Month title.
programme.sub-title: The Man With Two Red Shoes

programme.title: Mr. D: Gerry Has Hot Sub Anxiety
programme.desc: Gerry is blown away by the hotness of the new substitute teachers and sets his sights on ‘hot-sub Mary,’ the new love of his life.

programme.title: Mr. D: Busplotation
programme.desc: When Gerry hears that a Kickstarter campaign raised a bundle of money for a bullied bus monitor, he sets out to victimize himself in a similar fashion. Meanwhile, Bobbi comes to a surprising realization and Dwyer has a ‘killer’ new girlfriend.

programme.title: Mr. D: Gerry Charters A New Course
programme.desc: When Gerry isn’t given a full-time contract for next year, he makes a bold move that may change the course of his career.

programme.title: Mr. D: Gerry Wants to Make Phys Ed Great Again
programme.desc: After finally achieving his goal of teaching physical education full time, Gerry sets out to teach the course the way it should be taught.
programme.sub-title: Gerry Wants to Make Phys Ed Great Again

programme.title: Mr. D: Gerry Rigs PTA
programme.desc: Lisa runs for the unopposed Parent-Teacher Association president position as a way to punish the staff.

programme.title: Mr. D: Gerry Cuts a Footloose
programme.desc: When Gerry meets a DJ at the gym, he offers her a job at the school dance, but Robert has banned dances and won't budge on his position.

programme.title: Mr. D: Gerry Coaches Hockey
programme.desc: Gerry suspects Central High of cheating; Bobbi prepares for maternity leave.

programme.title: Mr. D: Formative Bullshit
programme.desc: Gerry discovers that teaching phys ed comes up a lot of paperwork; Bobbi tries to juggle work and a newborn; Lisa becomes friends with some moms who may have dirt on Xavier.

programme.title: Mr. D: Gerry Pay to Play
programme.desc: Gerry is in a compromising situation after the teacher auction; Emma enlists Dwyer to do repairs in the library; Trudy gets a surprise visit.

programme.title: Mr. D: Work to Rule
programme.desc: Gerry is in awe of the concept of work-to-rule; Lisa tries setting boundaries with her stepson; Nisha accidentally reveals too much to her students.

programme.title: Mr. D: Gerry Wants What He Can’t Trans
programme.desc: Gerry takes control of the girls' soccer team to get back at Coach Meyers; an ambitious mom seduces Frank; Lisa's reign of terror seemingly comes to an end.
programme.sub-title: Gerry Wants What He Can’t Trans

programme.title: Mr. D: Principal for a Day
programme.desc: Gerry's least-favorite student becomes Principal for a Day; a harmless prank goes sour; Bobbi accidentally ruins a student's life.

programme.title: Mr. D: Gerry’s Security Insecurity
programme.desc: Robert hires a security guard after a string of thefts; Robert learns the truth behind his baby's name.

programme.title: Scorpion: Strife on Mars
programme.desc: Team Scorpion gets trapped inside a failing and dangerous biodome designed for Mars while trying to extract a pair of scientists inside.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: The Forge of Creation
programme.desc: Lili, Vorjak, and the baby flee to find a shuttle to escape Earth. Liam and Sandoval are both tracking them - for different reasons. Augur is on the run from volunteers, but sends a friend (Juliet) to help Liam while he's gone.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: First Breath
programme.desc: A college friend of Renee's resurfaces and leads them on an investigation of the link between the Taelons and a biomedical facility's cloning operation.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Sins of the Father
programme.desc: A Senator's daughter is found to have kryss in her system. Liam and Renee suspect the Taelons lied about synthesizing it and there is a new location for production. They believe Federov is involved. Da'an is addicted to kryss.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Limbo
programme.desc: The Taelons lend some of their dwindling core energy to Joshua Doors for a pilot cold fusion project, but the energy is stolen, leaving the Taelons vulnerable. Jonathan Doors lives on as an AI in cyberspace.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Motherlode
programme.desc: Augur is caught and placed in a special prison where he is placed in a sensory deprivation chamber. An amoralist gets him out and wants Augur to help him steal the gold Zo'or has on the mothership. When Liam learns of what happened he tries to find Augur.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Take No Prisoners
programme.desc: The ANA negotiate amnesty for all resistance members, Liam tries to convince resistance member Halley Simmons to take the offer. Sandoval uses parasites to make volunteers do suicide missions against the resistance.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: The Second Wave
programme.desc: The Taelons decide to leave Earth when it appears that the Jiridians are invading. Meanwhile, a group of Resistance prisoners saved from the mother ship include several disguised Jiridians who are causing trouble for Liam and Renee.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Essence
programme.desc: Renee's brother has PTSD. In a desperate effort to help him, she agrees to let him undergo special treatment. The treatment removes his emotion. The project is overseen by Sandoval and the emotion they take is given to Da'an.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Phantom Companion
programme.desc: While on board the mothership, Renee sustains an injury and while being treated, she gets pulled into the walls and disappears.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Dream Stalker
programme.desc: Street asks Liam to help a friend who's arrested for the murder of a man he hated. Liam talks to him and he claims that he dreamed of killing him.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Lost Generation
programme.desc: A CVI malfunctions and kills a soon-to-be father. The investigation leads Liam and Renee to a fertility clinic. Renee finds out she is infertile and suspects that Taelon technology is affecting worldwide fertility.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: The Summit
programme.desc: Da'an leaves and doesn't being Liam along. Zo'or is frantic as to why Da'an left. Liam wants to help but Zo'or doesn't trust him because he doesn't have a CVI, so Liam agrees to be implanted.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Dark Matter
programme.desc: When the mother ship hits dark matter, the ship dos not begin to heal itself. Zo'or is left in an unconscious state and Sandoval takes control. He decides it is time to liberate the Earth from the Taelons.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Keys to the Kingdom
programme.desc: The relic left by Ma'el is stolen from the mother ship. Zo'or threatens to destroy a major city if it isn't returned. Liam believes the final chapters contain information on synthesizing core energy.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Street Chase
programme.desc: Sandoval is watching via nanobots inside Street as she tries to decipher Ma'el's riddle. She tries to figure out how to escape from Sandoval and the Taelons forcing her to solve the riddle. She flashes back to childhood fears.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Trapped by Time
programme.desc: Liam and Renee learn of a facility where time travelers from the future are being held. They try to learn from them what happens to the Taelons. Sandoval's wife is used as a pawn to gain important information from him.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Atonement
programme.desc: Sandoval goes to Renee to request a trial to testify to atrocities against humanity committed by the Taelons. A manhunt for Sandoval begins when he quits and leaves the mother ship. Liam has to protect him on his way to the grand jury.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Blood Ties
programme.desc: Strange assaults and several deaths may be related to a Doors/Taelon joint venture where Taelon core energy was used to heal people. It appears that the energy is being sucked back out of the patients.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Hearts and Minds
programme.desc: When Renee's boyfriend appears to her while he is imprisoned on the mother ship, Liam theorizes a special energy bond between the pair. Zo'or is upset by the human's betrayal and bans all humans from the mother ship.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Epiphany
programme.desc: The Taelons announce that they will all go into death stasis and wait on the mother ship for humans to discover how to create core energy and revive them. Zo'or and Da'an don't trust each other to be the last to submit to stasis.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Dark Horizons
programme.desc: Eight Taelons are killed when protectors' implants are re-calibrated with a new imperative to kill instead of protect Taelons. They fear the Jiridians have found a way to control the CVIs. Meanwhile, their core energy continues to deplete.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Point of No Return
programme.desc: When Street solves the 8th dimension of Ma'el's riddle, a beacon leads Liam to a chamber with a healing pool. However, there is only enough in the healing pool to help 6 Taelons. Zo'or will do anything to insure his own life.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Bond girls Honor Blackman and Britt Ekland hit the road in their Aston Martin to relive some 007 classics with Charlie Ross and Charles Hanson. They're out to make a profit as they roar toward an auction in Greenwich, London.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Father and son team Dan and Peter Snow go head-to-head with Charlie Ross and Charles Hanson on the hunt for the best antique deals as they travel through Hampshire and along the south coast, ending at an auction in Billingshurst, Sussex.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Sir Terry Wogan and Caroline Quentin add a touch of class as they seek profits to donate to Children In Need. In a classic 1948 Jaguar MK 4 they tour Oxfordshire with Charlie Ross and Charles Hanson toward a showdown in Chiswick, London.
programme.sub-title: Sir Terry Wogan and Caroline Quentin

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: British treasures Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie team up with experts Catherine Southon and James Lewis. They trawl the UK for fabulous antiques, from Potters Bar, through Hampstead and ending at auction in Heathfield, East Sussex.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: TV presenters Fiona Phillips and Richard Madeley go head-to-head with the help of experts Charles Hanson and James Braxton, in search of antique treasures. They travel the entire length of Cornwall, from Lostwithiel to auction in Bude.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Dancers and rivals, Arlene Phillips and Anton du Beke compete with the help of experts James Braxton and Charles Hanson, touring West Yorkshire, a traditional clog-making business and wonderful Blackpool Tower before ending up in Liverpool.
programme.sub-title: Arlene Phillips and Anton du Beke

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Old friends Michael Aspel and Una Stubbs hit the road with their antiques experts, Thomas Plant and James Lewis. They're hunting for the best antique deals, traveling through the delightful county of Norfolk, to an auction in King's Lynn.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Actors Alison Steadman and Larry Lamb add drama to the hunt for profits, as they team up with antiques experts Thomas Plant and Paul Laidlaw. Their trip begins in Arundel, then cruises along the Sussex coast to an auction at Eastbourne.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Jon Culshaw and Debra Stephenson team up with experts Thomas Plant and Mark Stacey, hunting antique bargains in and around Edinburgh before auction in Essex. Who will win the day? Hopefully both teams, as all profits go to Children in Need.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Eastenders veterans Pam St Clement and Rudolph Walker team up with experts Charles Hanson and James Braxton. They seek out antiques from East Sussex, Tumbridge, Wells in Kent, and finish up--dramatically--at auction at Rayleigh in Essex.
programme.sub-title: Pam St. Clement and Rudolph Walker

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Goalkeepers Peter Shilton and Peter Schmeichel buy and sell antiques around Cheshire, aided by Paul Laidlaw and Mark Stacey. They visit a Cycling Museum in Warrington and a Football Museum in Manchester, ending up at auction in Runcorn.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Silent Witness stars Emilia Fox and Tom Ward, aided by experts James Braxton and Charlie Ross, embark on a hunt for antiques to turn a profit. From Glasgow to the Village of Kilbarchan, they end at auction in Billingshurst in West Sussex.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Masterchef's Gregg Wallace and John Torode feel the heat as they team up with experts Charles Hanson and Charlie Ross. They visit stately homes on their way from Alton in Hampshire to auction at Fernhurst on the Surrey/West Sussex border.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Sue Holderness and John Challis bargain hunt with Charles Hanson and Philip Serrell in Essex for auction in Kent. Can John outsmart his TV wife or will Sue win big? Hopefully, both teams will do well as all profits go to Children in Need.
